49.21: these things hast thou done, and I have held my peace. Thou hast thought unjustly that I will be like thee: I will reprove thee, and set it against thy face.
49.22: Understand these things you that forget God: lest sometime he take you violently and there be none to deliver you.
49.23: The sacrifice of praise shall glorify me: and there is the way, by which I will shew him the salvation of God.

50.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David,
50.2: when Nathan the Prophet came to him, after that he had sinned with Bethsabee.
50.3: Have mercy on me, o God, according to thy great mercy. And according to the multitude of thy commiserations, take away mine iniquity.
50.4: Wash me more amply from mine iniquity: & cleanse me from my sin.
50.5: Because I do know mine iniquity: and my sin is before me always.
50.6: To thee only have I sinned, and have done evil before thee: that thou mayst be justified in thy words, and mayst overcome when thou art judged.
50.7: For behold I was conceived in iniquities: & my mother conceived me in sins.
50.8: For behold thou hast loved truth: the uncertain and hidden things of thy wisdom thou hast made manifest to me.
50.9: Thou shalt sprinkle me with hyssop, and I shall be cleansed: thou shalt wash me, and I shall be made whiter than snow.
50.10: To my hearing thou shalt give joy and gladness, and the bones humbled shall rejoice.
50.11: Turn away thy face from my sins: and wipe away all mine iniquities.
50.12: Create a clean heart in me, o God: and renew a right spirit in my bowels.
50.13: Cast me not away from thy face: and thy Holy spirit take not from me.
50.14: Render unto me the joy of thy salvation? And confirm me with the principal spirit.
50.15: I will teach the unjust thy ways: and the impious shall be converted to thee.
50.16: Deliver me from bloods, o God, the God of my salvation: and my tongue shall exult thy justice.
50.17: Lord thou wilt open my lips: & my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
50.18: Because if thou wouldest have had sacrifice, I had verily given it: with holocausts thou wilt not be delighted.
50.19: Asacrifice to God is an afflicted spirit: a contrite and humbled heart, o God, thou wilt not despise.
50.20: Deal favourably, o Lord, in thy good will with Sion: that the walls of Jerusalem may be built up.
50.21: Then shall thou accept sacrifice of justice, oblations, & holocausts: then shall they lay calves upon thine altar.

51.1: Unto the end, understanding to David,
51.2: When Doeg the Idumeite came and told Saul: David is come into the house of Achimelech.
51.3: Why doest thou glory in malice, which are mighty in iniquity?
51.4: All the day hath thy tongue thought injustice: as a sharp razor thou hast done guile.
51.5: Thou hast loved malice more than benignity: iniquity rather than to speak equity.
51.6: Thou hast loved all words of precipitation, a deceitful tongue.
51.7: Therefore will God destroy thee for ever, he will pluck thee out, & remove thee out of thy tabernacle: & thy root out of the land of the living.
51.8: The just shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him, and they shall say:
51.9: Behold the man, that hath not put God for his helper. But hath hoped in the multitude of his riches, and hath prevailed in his vanity.
51.10: But I as a fruitful olive tree in the house of God, have hoped in the mercy of God for ever: and for ever and ever.
51.11: I will confess to thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will expect thy name, because it is good in the sight of thy Saints.

52.1: Unto the end, for Mæleth, understandings of David. The fool hath said in his heart: There is no God.
52.2: They are corrupt, and become abominable in iniquities: there is not that doth good.
52.3: God hath looked forth from heaven, upon the children of men: to see if there be that understandeth, or seeketh after God.
52.4: All have declined, they are become unprofitable together: there is not that doth good, no there is not one.
52.5: Shall they not all know that work iniquity, that devour my people as food of bread?
52.6: God they have not invocated: there have they trembled for fear, where no fear was. Because God hath dissipated the bones of them that please men: they are confounded, because God hath despised them.
52.7: Who will give out of Sion the salvation of Israel? When God shall convert the captivity of his people: Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

53.1: Unto the end, in songs understanding for David
53.2: when the Zipheites were come, and said to Saul: Is not David hid with us?
53.3: O God save me in thy name: and in thy strength judge me.
53.4: O God hear my prayer with thine ears: receive the words of my mouth.
53.5: Because strangers have risen up against me, & the strong have sought my soul: and they have not set God before their eyes.
53.6: For behold God helpeth me: and our Lord is the receiver of my soul.
53.7: Turn away the evils of mine enemies: and in thy truth destroy them.
53.8: I will voluntarily sacrifice to thee, and will confess to thy name o Lord, because it is good:
53.9: Because thou hast delivered me out of all tribulation: and mine eye hath looked down upon mine enemies.

54.1: Unto the end in songs, understanding to David.
54.2: Hear my prayer, o God, despise not my petition.
54.3: Attend to me and hear me.
54.4: I am made sorrowful in my exercise: and am troubled at the voice of the enemy: and at the tribulation of the sinner. Because they have wrested iniquities upon me: & in anger they were troublesome to me.
54.5: My heart is troubled in me: & the fear of death is fallen upon me.
54.6: Fear and trembling are come upon me: and darkness hath covered me.
54.7: And I said: Who will give me wings as of a dove, and I will fly: and rest?
54.8: Lo I have gone far flying away: and I abode in the wilderness.
54.9: I expected him, that saved me from pusillanimity of spirit, and tempest.
54.10: Precipitate, o Lord, and divide their tongues: because I have seen iniquity, and contradiction in the city.
54.11: Day and night shall iniquity compass it upon the walls thereof, and labour in the midst thereof, and injustice.
54.12: And there hath not ceased out of the streets thereof usury and guile.
54.13: For if mine enemy had spoken evil to me, I would verily have born it. And if he that hated me had spoken great things upon me: I would perhaps have hid myself from him.
54.14: But thou a man of the same mind: my guide, and my familiar.
54.15: Which didest take sweet meats together with me: in the house of God we walked with consent.
54.16: Let death come upon them: and let them go down quick into hell. Because there is wickedness in their habitations, in the midst of them.
54.17: But I have cried to God, and our Lord will save me.
54.18: In the evening, and morning, & at midday, I will speak, and declare, and he will hear my voice.
54.19: He will redeem my soul in peace from them, that approach to me: because among many they were with me.
54.20: God will hear, and he which is before the worlds will humble them.
54.21: For there is no change with them, & they feared not God: he hath stretched forth his hand in repaying.
54.22: They have contaminated his testament, they are divided by the wrath of his countenance; and his heart hath approached. His words are made softer than oil: and the same are darts.
54.23: Cast thy care upon our Lord: and he will nourish thee: he will not give fluctuation to the just for ever.
54.24: But thou, o God, wilt bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloody and deceitful men shall not live half their days, but I will hope in thee, o Lord.

55.1: Unto the end, for a people, that is made far from the Saints, David in the inscription of the title, when the foreigners held him in Geth.
55.2: Have mercy on me, o God, because man hath trodden upon me: all the day impugning he hath afflicted me.
55.3: Mine enemies have trodden upon me all the day: because they are many that war against me.
55.4: From the height of the day I shall fear: but I will trust in thee.
55.5: In God I will praise my words, in God have I hoped: I will not fear what flesh may do to me.
55.6: All the day did they detest my words; against me all their cogitations are unto evil.
55.7: They will inhabit and keep secret: they will observe my heel. As they have expected my soul,
55.8: for nothing shalt thou save them: in wrath thou wilt break peoples. O God,
55.9: I have shewed my life to thee: thou hast set my tears in thy sight. As also in thy promise:
55.10: then shall mine enemies be turned backward. In what day soever I shall invocate thee: lo I have known that thou art my God.
55.11: In God I will praise the word, in our Lord will I praise thee saying: I have hoped in God, I will not fear what man can do to me.
55.12: In me, o God are thy vows, which I will render, praises to thee.
55.13: Because thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling: that I may please before God, in the light of the living.

56.1: Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when he fled from the face of Saul into the cave.
56.2: Have mercy on me, o God, have mercy on me: because my soul hath trusted in thee. And I will hope in the shadow of thy wings, until iniquity pass.
56.3: I will cry to God the highest: God that hath done me good.
56.4: He sent from heaven, and delivered me: he hath given into reproach them that trod upon me. God hath sent his mercy and his truth,
56.5: and hath delivered my soul out of the midst of lions' whelps: I slept troubled. The sons of men, their teeth are weapons and arrows: and their tongue a sharp sword.
56.6: Be exalted above the heavens, o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.
56.7: They prepared a snare for my feet: and bowed down my soul. They digged a pit before my face: and they are fallen into it.
56.8: My heart is ready, o God, my heart is ready: I will sing, and say a Psalm.
56.9: Arise my glory, arise psalter and harp: I will arise early.
56.10: I will confess to thee among peoples, o Lord: and I will say a Psalm to thee among the Gentiles.
56.11: Because thy mercy is magnified even to the heavens, and thy truth even to the clouds.
56.12: Be exalted above the heavens o God: and thy glory upon all the earth.

57.1: Unto the end, destroy not, to David, in the inscription of the title.
57.2: If in very deed you speak justice: judge right things ye sons of men.
57.3: For in the heart you work iniquities: in the earth your hands forge injustice.
57.4: Sinners are alienated from the matrice, they have erred from the womb: they have spoken false things.
57.5: They have fury according to the similitude of a serpent: as of the asp that is deaf, and stoppeth his ears.
57.6: Which will not hear the voice of the enchanters, and of the sorcerer enchanting wisely.
57.7: God shall break their teeth in their mouth, the cheek-tooth of the lions, our Lord will break in pieces.
57.8: They shall come to nothing as water running down: he hath bent his bow till they be weakened.
57.9: As wax that melteth, shall they be taken away: fire hath fallen on them, and they have not seen the sun.
57.10: Before your thorns did understand the old briar: as living so in wrath he swalloweth them.
57.11: The just shall rejoice when he shall see revenge: he shall wash his hands in the blood of a sinner.
57.12: And man shall say: If certes there be fruit to the just: there is a God certes judging them on the earth.

58.1: Unto the end, destroy not, to David in the inscription of the title, when Saul sent, and watched his house to kill him.
58.2: Deliver me from mine enemies, o my God: and from them that rise up against me defend me.
58.3: Deliver me from them that work iniquity: and from bloody men save me.
58.4: Because lo they have taken my soul: the strong have fallen violently upon me.
58.5: Neither is it mine iniquity, nor my sin, o Lord: without iniquity have I run, and gone directly.
58.6: Rise up to meet me, and see: and thou, o Lord, the God of powers, God of Israel, attend to visit all nations: have no mercy on all that work iniquity.
58.7: They will return at evening: and they shall suffer famine as dogs, and shall compass the city.
58.8: Behold they will speak in their mouth, and a sword in their lips: because who hath heard?
58.9: And thou, o Lord wilt scorn them: thou wilt bring to nought all the nations.
58.10: I will keep my strength to thee: because thou art my receiver:
58.11: my God, thy mercy shall prevent me.
58.12: God will shew unto me concerning mine enemies, kill them not lest sometime my peoples forget. Disperse them in thy strength: and depose them my protector, o Lord.
58.13: The sin of their mouth, the word of their lips: and let them be taken in their pride. And for cursing and lying they shall be talked of
58.14: in consummation: in wrath of consummation and they shall not be. And they shall know that God will rule over Jacob: and over the ends of the earth.
58.15: They shall be turned at evening, and shall suffer famine as dogs: & shall compass the city.
58.16: They shall be dispersed to eat: and if they be not filled, they will murmur also.
58.17: But I will sing thy strength: and will exalt thy mercy in the morning. Because thou art become my receiver, and my refuge in the day of my tribulation.
58.18: My helper, I will sing to thee, because thou art God my receiver: my God, my mercy.

59.1: Unto the end, for them that shall be changed, in the inscription of the title to David himself, for doctrine,
59.2: when he set fire on Mesopotamia of Syria, and in Sobal, and Joab returned, and struck Idumæa in the valley of salt-pits twelve thousand.
59.3: O God thou hast repelled us, & hast destroyed us: thou wast angry and hast had mercy on us.
59.4: Thou hast moved the earth, and hast troubled it: heal the breaches thereof, because it is moved.
59.5: Thou hast shewed unto thy people hard things: thou hast made us drink the wine of compunction.
59.6: Thou hast given a signification to them that fear thee: that they fly from the face of the bow.
59.7: That thy beloved may be delivered, save me with thy right hand, and hear me.
59.8: God hath spoken in his holy: I shall rejoice, and shall divide Sichem: and shall measure the valley of tabernacles.
59.9: Galaad is mine, and Manasses is mine: and Ephraim the strength of my head.
59.10: Juda my king: Moab the pot of my hope. Into Edom I will stretch out my shoe: the foreigners are subject to me.
59.11: Who shall conduct me into a fenced city? Who shall conduct me even into Idumea?
59.12: Shall not thou, o God, that hast expelled us: and wilt not thou o God, go forth in our hosts?
59.13: Give us aid from tribulation: because man's salvation is vain.
59.14: In God we shall do strength: and he shall bring to nothing them that afflict us.

60.1: Unto the end, in hymns to David.
60.2: Hear, o God, my petition: attend to my prayer.
60.3: From the ends of the earth I have cried to thee: whilst my heart was in anguish, thou didst exalt me on a rock.
60.4: Thou hast conducted me, because thou art made my hope: a tower of strength from the face of the enemy.
60.5: Ishall inhabit in thy tabernacle for ever: I shall be protected in the covert of thy wings.
60.6: Because thou my God hast heard my prayer: thou hast given inheritance to those that fear thy name.
60.7: Thou wilt add days upon the days of the king: his years even unto the day of generation and generation.
60.8: He is permanent for ever in the sight of God: his mercy and truth who shall require?
60.9: So will I say a Psalm to thy name for ever and ever: that I may render my vows from day to day.

61.1: Unto the end, for Idithun a Psalm of David.
61.2: Shall not my soul be subject to God? For of him is my salvation.
61.3: For he is my God, and my saviour: my receiver, I shall be moved no more.
61.4: How long set you violently upon a man: you all do kill, as it were upon a wall that is leaning, and a wall shaken.
61.5: But yet they thought to repel my price. I ran in thirst: they blessed with their mouth, and cursed with their heart.
61.6: But yet my soul be thou subject to God: because my patience is from him.
61.7: Because he is my God, and my saviour: my helper I shall not remove.
61.8: In God is my salvation, and my glory: the God of my help, and my hope is in God.
61.9: Hope in him all ye the congregation of people: pour out your hearts before him: God is our helper for ever.
61.10: But yet the children of men are vain, the children of men are liars in balances: that they may deceive by vanity together.
61.11: Hope not in iniquity, and covet not robberies: if riches abound set not your heart upon them.
61.12: Once hath God spoken, these two things have I heard:
61.13: That power is God's, and mercy, o Lord, is to thee: because thou wilt render to every one according to his works.

62.1: A Psalm of David when he was in the desert of Juda.
62.2: O God my God to thee I watch, from the morning light. My soul hath thirsted to thee, my flesh to thee very many ways.
62.3: In a desert land, and inaccessible, and without water, so in the holy have I appeared to thee, that I might see thy strength, and thy glory.
62.4: Because thy mercy is better than lives, my lips shall praise thee.
62.5: So will I bless thee in my life: and in thy name I will lift up my hands.
62.6: As with marrow and fatness let my soul be filled: and my mouth shall praise with lips of exultation.
62.7: If I have been mindful of thee upon my bed, in the morning I will meditate on thee:
62.8: because thou hast been my helper.
62.9: And in the covert of thy wings I will rejoice, my soul hath cleaved after thee: thy right hand hath received me.
62.10: But they in vain have sought my soul, they shall enter into the inferior parts of the earth.
62.11: They shall be delivered into the hands of the sword, they shall be the portions of foxes.
62.12: But the king shall rejoice in God, all shall be praised that swear by him: because the mouth is stopped of those that speak wicked things.

63.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
63.2: Hear o God my prayer when I make petition: from the fear of my enemy deliver my soul.
63.3: Thou hast protected me from the assembly of the malignant: from the multitude of them that work iniquity.
63.4: Because they have sharpened their tongues as a sword: they have bent the bow a bitter thing,
63.5: that they may shoot in secrets at the immaculate.
63.6: Suddenly they will shoot at him, and will not fear: they have confirmed to themselves a wicked word. They have talked to hide snares: they have said, Who shall see them?
63.7: They have searched iniquities: they have failed searching with scrutiny.
63.8: Man shall come to a deep heart: and God shall be exalted. Children's arrows are made their wounds:
63.9: and their tongues are weakened against them. All that saw them were troubled:
63.10: and every man feared. And they shewed forth the works of God: and they understood his doings.
63.11: The just shall rejoice in our Lord, and shall hope in him, & all the right of heart shall be praised.

64.1: To the end, a Psalm of David, the Canticle of Jeremy, and Ezechiel, to the people of the transmigration, when they began to go forth.
64.2: An hymn, o God, becometh thee in Sion: & a vow shall be rendered to thee in Jerusalem.
64.3: Hear my prayer: all flesh shall come to thee.
64.4: The words of the wicked have prevailed upon us: and thou wilt be propitious to our impieties.
64.5: Blessed is he whom thou hast chosen and taken: he shall dwell in thy courts.
64.6: We shall be replenished in the goods of thy house: holy is thy temple, marvelous in equity. Hear us, o God our saviour, the hope of all the ends of the earth, and in the sea far.
64.7: Preparing mountains in thy strength, girded with might:
64.8: which troublest the depths of the sea, the sound of the waves thereof.
64.9: The Gentiles shall be troubled, and they that inhabit the borders shall be afraid of thy signs: the out-goings of the morning and evening thou shalt delight.
64.10: Thou hast visited the earth, and hast inebriated it: thou hast multiplied to enrich it. The river of God is replenished with waters, thou hast prepared their meat: because so is the preparation thereof.
64.11: Inebriate her rivers, multiply her fruits: in her drops she shall rejoice springing.
64.12: Thou wilt bless the crown of the year of thy goodness: and thy fields shall be replenished with plenty.
64.13: The beautiful places of the desert shall be fat: and the little hills shall be girded about with exultation.
64.14: The rams of the sheep are clothed, and the valleys shall abound with corn: they will cry, yea they will say an hymn.

65.1: Unto the end, a Canticle of resurrection. Make ye jubilation to God all the earth,
65.2: say a Psalm to his name: give glory to his praise.
65.3: Say ye to God: How terrible are thy works, o Lord! In the multitude of thy strength thine enemies shall lie to thee.
65.4: Let all the earth adore thee, and sing to thee: let it sing a Psalm to thy name.
65.5: Come ye, and see the works of God: terrible in counsels over the children of men.
65.6: Who turneth the sea into dry land, in the river they shall pass on foot, there we shall rejoice in him.
65.7: Who ruleth in his strength for ever his eyes look upon the gentiles: they that exasperate him let them not be exalted in themselves.
65.8: Ye Gentiles bless our God: and make the voice of his praise heard.
65.9: Who hath put my soul in life: and hath not given my feet to be moved.
65.10: Because thou hast proved us, o God: by fire thou hast tried us, as silver is tried.
65.11: Thou hast brought us into a snare, thou hast laid tribulations on our back: thou hast set men upon our heads.
65.12: We have passed through fire and water: and thou hast brought us out into refreshing.
65.13: I will go into thy house with holocausts: I will render thee my vows:
65.14: which my lips have distinguished. And my mouth hath spoken in my tribulation.
65.15: Holocausts with marrow will I offer to thee, with incense of rams: I will offer to thee oxen with buck goats.
65.16: Come ye, hear, and I will tell all ye that fear God, what great things he hath done for my soul.
65.17: To him have I cried with my mouth, and have exulted under my tongue.
65.18: If I have beheld iniquity in my heart, our Lord will not hear.
65.19: Therefore hath God heard, and hath attended to the voice of my petition.
65.20: Blessed be God who hath not removed my prayer, and his mercy from me.

66.1: Unto the end, in hymns, a Psalm of Canticle to David.
66.2: God have mercy upon us, and bless us: illuminate his countenance upon us, and have mercy on us.
66.3: That we may know thy way upon earth: in all nations thy salvation.
66.4: Let peoples, o God, confess to thee: let all peoples confess to thee.
66.5: Let nations be glad & rejoice: because thou judgest peoples in equity, and the nations in earth thou doest direct.
66.6: Let peoples, o God, confess to thee, let all peoples confess to thee:
66.7: the earth hath yielded her fruit. God, our God bless us,
66.8: God bless us: and let all the ends of the earth fear him.

67.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of Canticle, to David himself.
67.2: Let God arise, and let his enemies be dispersed, and let them that hate him fly from his face.
67.3: As smoke vanisheth, let them vanish away: as wax melteth at the presence of fire, so let sinners perish at the presence of God.
67.4: And let the just make merry, and rejoice in the sight of God: and let them be delighted in mirth.
67.5: Sing to God, say a Psalm to his name: make way to him, who mounteth upon the west, Lord is his name. Rejoice ye in his sight, they shall be troubled at the presence of him:
67.6: the father of orphans, and judge of widows. God in his holy place:
67.7: God that maketh men to inhabit of one manner in a house. That bringeth forth them that be bound, in strength, likewise them that exasperate, that dwell in sepulchres.
67.8: O God when thou wentest forth in the sight of thy people, when thou didst pass through the desert.
67.9: The earth was moved, and the heavens also distilled, at the presence of the God of Sina, at the face of the God of Israel.
67.10: Voluntary rain shalt thou separate, o God, to thine inheritance: and it was weakened, but thou hath perfiteth it.
67.11: Thy living creatures shall dwell in it: thou hast prepared in thy sweetness for the poor, o God.
67.12: Our Lord shall give the word to them that evangelize, with great power.
67.13: The King of hosts the beloved of the beloved: and to the beauty of the house, to divide the spoils.
67.14: If ye sleep among the midst of the lots, the wings of a dove laid over with silver, and the hinder parts of her back in the paleness of gold.
67.15: Whilst the heavenly discerneth kings over her, with snow they shall be made white in Selmon.
67.16: The mountain of God a fat mountain. A mountain curded as cheese, a fat mountain:
67.17: why suppose you curded mountains? A mountain, in which it hath well pleased God to dwell therein: for indeed our Lord will dwell even to the end.
67.18: The chariot of God is ten thousand fold, thousands of them that rejoice, our Lord in them, in Sina in the holy place.
67.19: Thou art ascended on high, thou hast taken captivity: thou hast received gifts in men: for even those that do not believe, our Lord God to inhabit.
67.20: Blessed be our Lord day by day: the God of our salvations will make us a prosperous journey.
67.21: Our God is the God of saving: and the issues of death are of our Lord, our Lord.
67.22: But yet God still break the heads of his enemies: the hairy crown of them, that walk in their sins.
67.23: Our Lord said: Out of Basan I will convert, I will convert into the depth of the sea.
67.24: That thy foot may be dipped in blood: the tongue of thy dogs (made red) with the same (blood) of the enemies.
67.25: They have seen thy enterings in, o God, the enterings of my God: of my King who is in the holy place.
67.26: Princes came before joined with them that sang, in the midst of young women playing on timbrels.
67.27: In Churches bless ye God our Lord, of the fountains of Israel.
67.28: There Benjamin a young man, in excess of mind. The Princes of Juda their leaders: the Princes of Zabulon, the Princes of Nephthali.
67.29: Command thy strength, o God: confirm this, o God, which thou hast wrought in us.
67.30: From thy temple in Jerusalem, kings shall offer gifts to thee.
67.31: Rebuke the wild beasts of the reed, the congregation of bulls in the kine of thy peoples: that they may exclude them, which are tried with silver. Dissipate the nations that will wars:
67.32: Legates shall come out of Ægypt: Æthiopia shall prevent his hands to God.
67.33: Ye Kingdoms of the earth sing to God: sing to our Lord:
67.34: Sing ye to God, that mounteth upon the heaven of heaven, to the East. Behold he will give to his voice the voice of strength:
67.35: give you glory to God upon Israel, his magnificence, and his power in the clouds.
67.36: God is marvelous in his Saints, the God of Israel he will give power and strength to his people, God be blessed.

68.1: Unto the end, for them that shall be changed, to David.
68.2: Save me, o God: because waters are entered into my soul.
68.3: I stick fast in the mire of the depth: and there is no sure standing. I am come into the depth of the sea: and a tempest hath overwhelmed me.
68.4: I have laboured crying, my jaws are made hoarse: my eyes have failed, whilst I hope in my God.
68.5: They are multiplied above the hairs of my head, that hate me without cause. Mine enemies are made strong, that have persecuted me unjustly: then did I pay the things that I took not.
68.6: O God thou knowest my foolishness: and mine offences are not hid from thee.
68.7: Let them not be ashamed upon me, which expected thee, o Lord, Lord of hosts. Let them not be confounded upon me that seek thee, o God of Israel.
68.8: Because for thee have I sustained reproach, confusion hath covered my face.
68.9: I am become a foreigner to my brethren, and a stranger to the sons of my mother.
68.10: Because the zeal of thy house hath eaten me: and the reproaches of them that reproached thee, fell upon me.
68.11: And I covered my soul in fasting: and it was made a reproach to me.
68.12: And I put hair cloth my garment, & I became a parable to them.
68.13: They spake against me that sat in the gate; and they sung against me that drank wine.
68.14: But I, my prayer to thee, o Lord: a time of thy good pleasure, o God. In the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation:
68.15: Deliver me out of the mire, that I stick not fast: deliver me from them that hate me, and from the depths of waters.
68.16: Let not the tempest of water drown me, nor the depth swallow me: neither let the pit shut his mouth upon me.
68.17: Hear me, o Lord, because thy mercy is benign: according to the multitude of thy commiserations have respect to me.
68.18: And turn not away thy face from thy servant: because I am in tribulation, hear me speedily.
68.19: Attend to my soul, and deliver it: because of mine enemies deliver me.
68.20: Thou knowest my reproach & my confusion, & my shame.
68.21: In thy sight are all they that afflict me, my heart hath looked for reproach and misery. And I expected somebody that would be sorry together with me, and there was none: and that would comfort me, and I found not.
68.22: And they gave gall for my meat: & in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
68.23: Let their table be made a snare before them, & for retributions, and for a scandal.
68.24: Let their eyes be darkened that they see not: and make their back crooked always.
68.25: Pour out thy wrath upon them: and let the fury of thy wrath overtake them.
68.26: Let their habitation be made desert: and in their tabernacles let there be none to dwell.
68.27: Because whom thou hast strucken, they have persecuted: and upon the sorrow of my wounds they have added.
68.28: Add thou iniquity upon their iniquity: and let them not enter into thy justice.
68.29: Let them be put out of the book of the living: and with the just let them not be written.
68.30: I am poor and sorrowful: thy salvation, o God, hath received me.
68.31: I will praise the name of God with canticle: and will magnify him in praise.
68.32: And it shall please God more than a young calf: that bringeth forth horns and hoofs.
68.33: Let the poor see and rejoice: seek ye God, and your soul shall live.
68.34: Because our Lord hath heard the poor: and he hath not despised his prisoners.
68.35: Let the heavens and earth praise him, the sea, and all the creeping beasts in them.
68.36: Because God will save Sion: and the cities of Juda shall be built up. And they shall inhabit there, & by inheritance, they shall get it.
68.37: And the seed of his servants shall possess it, and they that love his name shall dwell in it.

69.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David, in remembrance, that our Lord saved him.
69.2: O God intend unto my help: Lord make haste to help me.
69.3: Let them be confounded and be ashamed, that seek my soul.
69.4: Let them be turned away backward, and be ashamed that will me evils. Let them be turned away forthwith ashamed, that say to me: Well, well.
69.5: Let all that seek thee rejoice, and be glad in thee, and let them say always: Our Lord be magnified: which love thy salvation.
69.6: But I am needy and poor: O God help me, thou art my helper, and deliverer: o Lord be not slack.

70.1: A Psalm for David, of the sons of Jonadab, and the former captives. In thee o Lord I have hoped, let me not be confounded for ever:
70.2: in thy justice deliver me, and receive me. Incline thine ear to me and save me.
70.3: Be unto me for a God protector, and for a fenced place: that thou mayst save me: Because thou art my firmament, and my refuge.
70.4: My God deliver me out of the hand of the sinner, and out of the hand of him that doth against the law, and of the unjust.
70.5: Because thou art my patience, o Lord; o Lord my hope from my youth.
70.6: Upon thee have I been confirmed from the womb: from my mother's belly thou art my protector. In thee is my singing always:
70.7: I was made to many as a wonder: & thou art a strong helper.
70.8: Let my mouth be filled with praise, that I may sing thy glory: all the day thy greatness.
70.9: Reject me not in the time of old age: when my strength shall fail, forsake me not.
70.10: Because mine enemies have said to me: and they that watched my soul consulted together.
70.11: saying: God hath forsaken him, pursue, and take him: because there is none to deliver.
70.12: O God be not far from me: my God have respect to mine aid.
70.13: Let them be confounded & fail that detract from my soul: let them be covered with confusion and shame, that seek evils to me.
70.14: But I will always hope: and will add upon all thy praise.
70.15: My mouth shall shew forth thy justice: all the day thy salvation. Because I have not known learning, I will enter into the powers of our Lord:
70.16: O Lord I will be mindful of thy justice only.
70.17: O God thou hast taught me from my youth: and until now I will pronounce thy marvelous works.
70.18: And unto ancient age, and old age: o God, forsake me not? Until I shew forth thy arm to all the generation, that is to come. Thy might,
70.19: and thy justice, o God, even to the highest, great marvels, which thou hast done: O God who may be like to thee?
70.20: How great tribulations hast thou shewed me, many and evil: and turning thou hast quickened me: and from the depths of the earth thou hast brought me back again.
70.21: Thou hast multiplied thy magnificence: and being turned thou hast comforted me.
70.22: For I also will confess to thee in the instruments of Psalm thy truth: O God, I will sing to thee on the harp, holy one of Israel.
70.23: My lips shall rejoice when I shall sing to thee; and my soul which thou hast redeemed.
70.24: Yea and my tongue all the day shall meditate thy justice: when they shall be confounded and ashamed that seek evils to me.

71.2: O God give thy judgment to the king: and thy justice to the son of the king. To judge thy people in justice, and thy poor in judgment.
71.3: Let the mountains receive peace for the people: and the little hills justice.
71.4: He shall judge the poor of the people, and shall save the children of the poor: and he shall humble the calumniator.
71.5: And he shall continue with the sun, and before the moon in generation and generation.
71.6: He shall descend as rain upon a fleece: and as drops distilling upon the earth.
71.7: There shall rise in his days justice, and aboundance of peace, until the moon be taken away.
71.8: And he shall rule from sea unto sea: and from the river even to the ends of the round world.
71.9: Before him shall the Æthiopians fall down: and his enemies shall lick the earth.
71.10: The kings of Tharsis, and the Islands shall offer presents: the kings of the Arabians, and of Saba shall bring gifts.
71.11: And all kings of the earth shall adore him: all nations shall serve him.
71.12: Because he shall deliver the poor from the mighty: and the poor which had no helper.
71.13: He shall spare the poor and needy: and he shall save the souls of the poor.
71.14: From usuries and iniquity he shall redeem their souls: and their name shall be honourable before him.
71.15: And he shall live, and there shall be given him of the gold of Arabia, and they shall adore it always: all the day they shall bless him.
71.16: And there shall be a firmament in the earth in the tops of mountains, the fruit thereof shall be extolled far above Libanus: and they shall flourish of the city, as the grass of the earth.
71.17: Be his name blessed for ever: before the sun his name is permanent. And all the tribes of the earth shall be blessed in him: all nations shall magnify him.
71.18: Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel, who only doth marvelous things.
71.19: And blessed be the name of his majesty for ever: and all the earth shall be filled with his majesty. Be it, be it.
71.20: The praises of David, the son of Jesse are ended.

72.1: A Psalm to Asaph. How good is God to Israel, to them that are of a right heart!
72.2: But my feet were almost moved, my steps almost slipped.
72.3: Because I have had zeal upon the wicked, seeing the peace of sinners.
72.4: Because there is no respect to their death: and stability in their plague.
72.5: In the labours of men they are not, and with men they shall not be scourged.
72.6: Therefore hath pride held them, they are covered with their iniquity and impiety.
72.7: Their iniquity hath proceeded as it were of fat: they have passed into the affection of the heart.
72.8: They have thought and have spoken wickedness: they have spoken iniquity on high.
72.9: They have set their mouth unto heaven: and their tongue hath passed in the earth.
72.10: Therefore will my people return here: and full days shall be found in them.
72.11: And they have said: How doth God know, and is there knowledge in the highest?
72.12: Lo the sinners themselves, and they that abound in the world, have obtained riches.
72.13: And I said: Then have I justified my heart without cause, and have washed my hands amongst innocents:
72.14: And have been scourged all the day, and my chastising in the mornings.
72.15: If I said: I will speak this: behold I reproved the nation of thy children.
72.16: I thought to know this thing, it is labour before me:
72.17: Until I may enter into the sanctuary of God: and may understand concerning their latter ends.
72.18: But yet for guiles thou hast put it to them: thou hast cast them down whilst they were elevated.
72.19: How are they brought into desolation, they have failed suddenly: they have perished for their iniquity.
72.20: As the dream of them that rise, o Lord, in thy city thou shalt bring their image to nothing.
72.21: Because my heart is inflamed, and my reins are changed:
72.22: And I am brought to nothing, and knew not.
72.23: As a beast am I become with thee: and I always with thee.
72.24: Thou hast held my right hand: and in thy will thou hast conducted me, and with glory thou hast received me.
72.25: For what is to me in heaven? And besides thee what would I upon the earth?
72.26: My flesh hath fainted, and my heart: God of my heart, and God my portion for ever.
72.27: For behold, they that make themselves far from thee, shall perish: thou hast destroyed all that fornicate from thee.
72.28: But it is good for me to cleave to God: to put my hope in our Lord God. That I may shew forth all thy praises, in the gates of the daughter of Sion.

73.1: Understanding to Asaph. Why hast thou, o God, repelled for ever: is thy fury wrath upon the sheep of thy pasture?
73.2: Be mindful of thy congregation, which thou hast possessed from the beginning. Thou hast redeemed the rod of thine inheritance: mount Sion, in which thou hast dwelt.
73.3: Lift up thy hands upon their prides for ever: how great things hath the enemy done malignantly in the holy place?
73.4: And they that hate thee have gloried: in the midst of thy solemnity. They have set their signs, for signs:
73.5: and have not known, as in the issue on high. As in a wood of trees they have with axes
73.6: cut out the gates thereof together: in hatchet and chip-axe they have cast it down.
73.7: They have burnt thy sanctuary with fire: they have polluted the tabernacle of thy name in the earth.
73.8: Their kindred together have said in their heart: Let us make all the festival days of God to cease from the earth.
73.9: Our signs we have not seen, there is now no Prophet: and he will know us no more.
73.10: How long, o God, shall the enemy upbraid: the adversary provoke thy name for ever?
73.11: Why doest thou turn away thy hand, and thy right hand, out of the midst of thy bosom for ever?
73.12: But God our King before the worlds: he hath wrought salvation in the midst of the earth.
73.13: Thou in thy strength hast confirmed the sea: thou hast crushed the head of Dragons in the waters.
73.14: Thou hast broken the heads of the dragon: thou hast given him for meat to the peoples of the Æthiopians.
73.15: Thou hast broken up fountains, and torrents, thou hast dried the rivers of Ethan.
73.16: The day is thine, and the night is thine: thou hast made the morning and the sun.
73.17: Thou hast made all the coasts of the earth: the summer and the spring thou hast formed them.
73.18: Be mindful of this, the enemy hath upbraided our Lord: and a foolish people hath provoked thy name.
73.19: Deliver not to beasts the souls that confess to thee: and the souls of the poor forget not for ever.
73.20: Have respect unto thy testament: because they that are obscure of the earth, are filled with houses of iniquities.
73.21: Let not the humble be turned away being confounded: the poor and needy shall praise thy name.
73.22: Arise God, judge thy cause: be mindful of those thy reproaches, that are from the foolish man all the day.
73.23: Forget not the voices of thine enemies: the pride of them that hate thee, hath ascended always.

74.1: Unto the end, Corrupt not, a Psalm of Canticle to Asaph.
74.2: We will confess to thee, o God: we will confess, and will invocate thy name. We will tell thy marvelous works:
74.3: when I shall take a time, I will judge justices.
74.4: The earth is melted, and all that dwell in it: I have confirmed the pillars thereof.
74.5: Isaid to the wicked: Do not wickedly; and to them that offend: Exalt not the horn.
74.6: Exalt not your horn on high: speak not iniquity against God.
74.7: For neither from the East, nor from the West, nor from the desert mountains:
74.8: because God is Judge. This man he humbleth, and him he exalteth:
74.9: because there is a cup in the hand of our Lord of mere wine full of mixture. And he hath poured it out of this into that, but yet the dregs thereof are not emptied: all the sinners of the earth shall drink.
74.10: But I will shew forth for ever: I will sing to the God of Jacob.
74.11: And I will break all the horns of sinners: and the horns of the just shall be exalted.

75.1: Unto the end, in praises, a Psalm to Asaph, a Canticle to the Assyrians.
75.2: God is known in Jewry: in Israel his name is great.
75.3: And his place is made in peace; and his habitation in Sion.
75.4: There he brake the powers of bows, the shield, the sword, and the battle.
75.5: Thou doest illuminate marvelously from the eternal mountains:
75.6: all the foolish of heart were troubled. They slept their sleep: and all the men of riches found nothing in their hands.
75.7: At thy reprehension, o God of Jacob, they have all slumbered that mounted on horses.
75.8: Thou art terrible, and who shall resist thee? From that time thy wrath.
75.9: From heaven thou hast made thy judgment heard: the earth trembled and was quiet.
75.10: When God arose unto judgment, that he might save all the meek of the earth.
75.11: Because the cogitation of man shall confess to thee: and the remains of the cogitation shall keep festival day to thee.
75.12: Vow ye and render to our Lord your God: all ye that round about him bring gifts. To the terrible,
75.13: and him that taketh away the spirit of Princes, terrible to the kings of the earth.

76.1: Unto the end, for Idithun, a psalm to Asaph.
76.2: With my voice I have cried to our Lord: with my voice to God and he attended to me.
76.3: In the day of my tribulation I sought God, with my hands in the night before him: and I was not deceived. My soul refused to be comforted,
76.4: I was mindful of God and was delighted, and was exercised: and my spirit fainted.
76.5: Mine eyes prevented the watches: I was troubled & spake not.
76.6: Ithought upon old days: and the eternal years I had in mind.
76.7: And I meditated in the night with my heart, and I was exercised, and I swept my spirit.
76.8: Why, will God reject for ever: or will he not add to be better pleased as yet?
76.9: Or will he cut off his mercy for ever, from generation unto generation?
76.10: Or will God forget to have mercy? Or will he in his wrath keep in his mercies?
76.11: And I said: Now have I begun; this is the change of the right hand of the Highest.
76.12: I have been mindful of the works of our Lord: because I will be mindful from the beginning of thy marvelous works.
76.13: And I will meditate in all thy works: and in thy inventions I will be exercised.
76.14: O God in the holy is thy way: what god is great as our God?
76.15: thou art the God that doest marvelous things. Thou hast made thy power known among peoples:
76.16: thou hast with thine arm redeemed thy people, the children of Jacob, and Joseph.
76.17: The waters saw thee, o God, the waters saw thee: and they were afraid, and the depths were troubled.
76.18: A multitude of the sounding of waters: the clouds gave a voice. For indeed arrows do pass:
76.19: the voice of thy thunder in a wheel. Thy lightnings shined to the round world: the earth was moved and troubled.
76.20: Thy way in the sea, and thy paths in many waters: and thy steps shall not be known.
76.21: Thou hast conducted thy people as sheep: in the hand of Moyses and Aaron.

77.1: Understanding to Asaph. My people attend ye to my law: incline your ear unto the words of my mouth.
77.2: I will open my mouth in parables: I will speak propositions from the beginning.
77.3: How great things have we heard and have known them, and our fathers have told us.
77.4: They were not hid from their children, in another generation. Telling the praises of our Lord, and his powers, and his marvelous works which he hath done.
77.5: And he raised up a testimony in Jacob: and made a law in Israel. How great things he commanded our fathers, to make the same known to their children:
77.6: that another generation may know. The children that shall be born, and shall rise up, and shall tell their children.
77.7: That they may put their hope in God, and may not forget the works of God: and may seek after his commandments.
77.8: That they become not as their fathers: a perverse generation and exasperating. A generation, that hath not directed their heart, their spirit hath not been faithful towards God.
77.9: The children of Ephrem bending, and shooting with bow: were turned in the day of battle.
77.10: They kept not the testament of God: and in his law they would not walk.
77.11: And they forgat his benefits, and his marvelous works, which he shewed them.
77.12: Before their fathers he did marvelous things in the land of Ægypt, in the field of Tanis.
77.13: He divided the sea & brought them through: and he made the waters to stand as in a bottle.
77.14: And he conducted them in a cloud by day: and all the night by light of fire.
77.15: He struck the rock in the desert: and gave them water to drink as in a great depth.
77.16: And he brought forth water out of the rock: and made waters run down as rivers.
77.17: And they added as yet to sin unto him: they provoked the Highest to wrath in the place without water.
77.18: And they tempted God in their hearts: so that they asked meats for their lives.
77.19: And they spake evil of God, they said: Can God prepare a table in the desert?
77.20: Because he struck the rock and waters ran, & torrents flowed: Can he also give bread, or prepare a table for his people?
77.21: Therefore our Lord heard, and made delay: and fire was kindled in Jacob, and wrath ascended upon Israel.
77.22: Because they believed not in God, nor hoped in his salvation.
77.23: And he commanded the clouds from above, and opened the gates of heaven.
77.24: And he rained them Manna to eat, and bread of heaven he gave to them.
77.25: Bread of Angels did man eat: he sent them victuals in aboundance.
77.26: He transported the South-wind from heaven: and in his power he brought in the South-west-wind.
77.27: And he rained upon them flesh as dust: and as the sand of the sea feathered fowls.
77.28: And they fell in the midst of their camp: about their tabernacles.
77.29: And they did eat and were filled exceedingly, and their desire he brought to them:
77.30: They were not defrauded of their desire. As yet their meats were in their mouth:
77.31: And the wrath of God ascended upon them. And he killed their fat ones, and the chosen of Israel he hindered.
77.32: In all these things they sinned as yet: and they believed not in his marvelous works.
77.33: And their days failed in vanity: and their years in haste.
77.34: When he slew them, they sought him: and they returned, and early they came to him.
77.35: And they remembered that God is their helper: and the high God is their redeemer.
77.36: And they loved him with their mouth, and with their tongue they did lie to him.
77.37: But their heart was not right with him: neither were they counted faithful in his testament.
77.38: But he is merciful, and will be propitious to their sins: and he will not destroy them. And he abounded to turn away his wrath: and he kindled not all his wrath.
77.39: And he remembered that they are flesh: spirit going, and not returning.
77.40: How often have they exasperated him in the desert: provoked him to wrath in the place without water.
77.41: And they returned, and tempted God: and the holy one of Israel they exasperated.
77.42: They did not remember his hand: in the day that he redeemed them from the hand of the afflicter.
77.43: As he put his signs in Ægypt, and his wonders in the field of Tanis.
77.44: And he turned their rivers into blood, & their showers that they might not drink.
77.45: He sent upon them a swarm of flies, and it eat them: and the frog, and it destroyed them.
77.46: And he gave their fruits to the blast, and their labours to the locust.
77.47: And he killed their vineyards with hail: and their mulberry trees with hoar-frost.
77.48: And he delivered their beast to hail: and their possession to fire.
77.49: He sent upon them the wrath of his indignation: indignation, & wrath, and tribulation: immissions by evil angels.
77.50: He made a way to the path of his wrath, he spared not their lives from death: and their cattle he shut up in death.
77.51: And he struck all the first-born in the land of Ægypt: the first fruits of all their labours in the tabernacles of Cham.
77.52: And he took away his people as sheep: and led them as a flock in the desert.
77.53: And he brought them forth in hope, and they feared not: and the sea covered their enemies.
77.54: And he brought them into the mount of his sanctification, the mount, which his right hand purchased. And he cast out the gentiles from their face: and by lot he divided the land of them in a cord of distribution:
77.55: And he made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tabernacles.
77.56: And they tempted, and exasperated God the highest, and they kept not his testimonies.
77.57: And they turned away themselves, & kept not the covenant: even as their fathers, they were turned as a crooked bow.
77.58: They incensed him to wrath in their hills: and in their gravens they provoked him to emulation.
77.59: God heard, and contemned: and he brought Israel to nothing exceedingly.
77.60: And he rejected the tabernacle of Silo, his tabernacle, where he dwelt among men.
77.61: And he delivered their force into captivity: and their beauty into the hands of the enemy.
77.62: And he shut up his people in the sword: and he despised his inheritance.
77.63: Fire devoured their young men: and their virgins were not lamented.
77.64: Their Priests fell by the sword: and their widows were not wept for.
77.65: And our Lord was raised up as one that sleepeth: as a mighty man having surfeited of wine.
77.66: And he struck his enemies on the hinder parts: an everlasting reproach he gave to them.
77.67: And he rejected the tabernacle of Joseph: and the tribe of Ephraim he chose not.
77.68: But he chose the tribe of Juda, mount Sion which he loved.
77.69: And he built his sanctuary as of unicorns in the land, which he hath founded for ever.
77.70: And he chose David his servant, and took him from the flocks of sheep: from after the ewes with young he took him.
77.71: To feed Jacob his servant, and Israel his inheritance.
77.72: And he fed them in the innocency of his heart: and in the understandings of his hands he conducted them.

78.1: A Psalm to Asaph. O God the Gentiles are come into thine inheritance, they have polluted thy holy temple: they have made Jerusalem as a watch tower of fruits.
78.2: They have made the carcasses of thy servants, meats for the fowls of the air: the flesh of thy Saints for the beasts of the land.
78.3: They have poured out their blood as water round about Jerusalem: and there was none to bury them.
78.4: We are become a reproach to our neighbours: a scorn and mock to them that are round about us.
78.5: How long, o Lord, wilt thou be angry for ever: shall thy zeal be kindled as a fire?
78.6: Pour out thy wrath upon the Gentiles, that have not known thee: & upon the Kingdoms, that have not invocated thy name.
78.7: Because they have devoured Jacob: and his place they have made desolate.
78.8: Remember not our old iniquities, let thy mercies quickly prevent us: because we are become exceeding poor.
78.9: Help us, o God our Saviour: and for the glory of thy name o Lord deliver us: and be propitious to our sins for thy name's sake.
78.10: Lest they say perhaps amongst the Gentiles: where is their God? And be notified in the nations before our eyes. The revenge of the blood of thy servants, which hath been shed:
78.11: let the groaning of the fettered enter in thy sight. According to the greatness of thine arm possess thou the children of them that are put to death.
78.12: And render to our neighbours sevenfold in their bosom: their reproach wherewith they have reproached thee, o Lord.
78.13: But we thy people, and the sheep of thy pasture, will confess to thee for ever. Unto generation and generation we will shew forth thy praise.

79.1: Unto the end, for them that shall be changed, testimony to Asaph, a Psalm.
79.2: Thou that rulest Israel, attend: that conductest Joseph as a sheep. Which sittest upon the Cherubs, be manifest
79.3: before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasses. Raise up thy might, and come to save us.
79.4: O God convert us: and shew thy face, & we shall be saved.
79.5: O Lord the God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry upon the prayer of thy servant?
79.6: Thou wilt feed us with the bread of tears: and give us drink with tears in measure.
79.7: Thou hast made us to be a contradiction to our neighbours: and our enemies have scorned us.
79.8: O God of hosts convert us: and shew thy face, and we shall be saved.
79.9: Thou didst transport the vineyard out of Ægypt: thou didst cast out the Gentiles, and plantedst it.
79.10: Thou wast the guide of the way, in the sight thereof: thou didst plant the roots thereof, and it filled the earth.
79.11: The shadow of it covered the mountains: and the boughs of it the cedars of God.
79.12: It extended her branches even to the sea; and her boughs unto the river.
79.13: Why hast thou destroyed the hedge thereof: and all that pass by the way do pluck it.
79.14: The boar of the wood hath destroyed it: and the singular wild beast hath eaten it.
79.15: O God of hosts return: regard from heaven, and see, and visit this vineyard.
79.16: And perfit it, which thy right hand hath planted: and upon the son of man whom thou hast confirmed to thee.
79.17: Things burnt with fire, and digged down at the increpation of thy countenance shall perish.
79.18: Let thy hand be upon the man of thy right hand: and upon the son of man, whom thou hast confirmed to thee.
79.19: And we depart not from thee, thou wilt quicken us: and we will invocate thy name.
79.20: O Lord the God of hosts convert us: and shew thy face, and we shall be saved.

80.1: Unto the end, for wine presses, a Psalm to Asaph himself.
80.2: Rejoice to God our helper: make Jubilation to the God of Jacob.
80.3: Take ye Psalm, and give timbrel: pleasant Psalter with the harp.
80.4: Sound ye with trumpet in the new moon, in the notable day of your solemnity.
80.5: Because it is a precept in Israel, and judgment to the God of Jacob.
80.6: He put it for a testimony in Joseph, when he came out of the Land of Ægypt: he heard a tongue which he knew not.
80.7: He turned away his back from burdens: his hands served in baskets.
80.8: In tribulation thou didst invocate me, and I heard thee: I heard thee in the secret of the tempest: I proved thee at the water of contradiction.
80.9: Hear, o my people, and I will contest thee: Israel if thou wilt hear me.
80.10: There shall be no new god in thee, neither shalt thou adore a strange god.
80.11: For I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Ægypt: dilate thy mouth and I will fill it.
80.12: And my People heard not my voice: and Israel attended not to me.
80.13: And I let them alone, according to the desires of their heart, they shall go in their own inventions.
80.14: If my people had heard me, if Israel had walked in my ways:
80.15: I had for nothing perhaps humbled their enemies: and had laid my hand upon those that afflict them.
80.16: The enemies of our Lord have lied to him: and their time shall be for ever.
80.17: And he fed them of the fat of corn: and out of the rock with honey he filled them.

81.1: A Psalm to Asaph. God stood in the assembly of gods, and in the midst he judgeth gods.
81.2: How long judge ye iniquity: and accept ye the persons of sinners.
81.3: Judge ye for the needy and the pupil: justify ye the humble, and the poor.
81.4: Take away the poor: and deliver the needy from the hand of the sinner.
81.5: They knew not, neither did they understand, they walk in darkness: all the foundations of the earth shall be moved.
81.6: Isaid: You are gods, and the sons of the highest all.
81.7: But you shall die as men: and fall as one of the Princes.
81.8: Arise, o God, judge the earth: because thou shalt inherit in all the Gentiles.

82.1: A Canticle of Psalm to Asaph.
82.2: O God who shall be like to thee? Hold not thy peace, neither be thou appeased, o God:
82.3: for behold thine enemies have made a sound: they that hate thee have lifted up the head.
82.4: They have taken malignant counsel upon thy people: and they have devised against thy Saints.
82.5: They have said: Come, and let us destroy them out of the nation: and let the name of Israel be remembered no more.
82.6: Because they have devised with one consent: they have together made a covenant against thee.
82.7: The tabernacles of the Idumeians, and the Ismahelites, Moab, and the Agarens.
82.8: Gebal, and Ammon, Amalec: the foreigners, with the inhabitants of Tyre.
82.9: Yea and Assur also is come with them: they are made an aid to the children of Lot.
82.10: Do to them as to Madian, and Sisara: as to Jabin in the torrent Cisson.
82.11: They perished in Endor: they were made as the dung of the earth.
82.12: Put their Princes as Oreb, and Zeb, and Zebee, and Salmana. All their Princes:
82.13: which have said: Let us possess the Sanctuary of God for an inheritance.
82.14: My God put them as a wheel: and as stubble before the face of the wind.
82.15: Even as fire that burneth a wood: & as a flame that burneth the mountains.
82.16: So shalt thou pursue them in thy tempest: and in thy wrath thou shalt trouble them.
82.17: Fill their faces with ignominy: and they will seek thy name, o Lord.
82.18: Let them be ashamed, and troubled for ever and ever: and let them be confounded, and perish.
82.19: And let them know that Lord is thy name, thou only the Highest in all the earth.

83.1: Unto the end, for wine presses, to the children of Core, a Psalm.
83.2: How beloved are thy tabernacles, o Lord of hosts!
83.3: my soul coveteth and fainteth unto the courts of our Lord. My heart and my flesh have rejoiced toward the living God.
83.4: For the sparrow also hath found her an house: and the turtle-dove a nest for herself, where she may lay her young ones. Thine altars, o Lord of hosts: my King, and my God.
83.5: Blessed are they that dwell in thy house, o Lord: for ever and ever they shall praise thee.
83.6: Blessed is the man, whose help is from thee: he hath disposed ascensions in his heart,
83.7: in the vale of tears, in the place which he hath appointed.
83.8: For the law-giver shall give blessing, they shall go from virtue into virtue: the God of gods shall be seen in Sion.
83.9: Lord God of hosts hear my prayer: receive with thine ear, o God of Jacob.
83.10: Behold, o God our protector: and look upon the face of thy Christ.
83.11: Because better is one day in thy courts, above thousands. I have chosen to be an abject in the house of my God: rather than to dwell in the tabernacles of sinners.
83.12: Because God loveth mercy and truth: our Lord will give grace and glory.
83.13: He will not deprive them of good things, that walk in innocency: o Lord of hosts, blessed is the man that hopeth in thee.

84.1: Unto the end, to the children of Core, a Psalm.
84.2: O Lord thou hast blessed thy land: thou hast turned away the captivity of Jacob.
84.3: Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people: thou hast covered all their sins.
84.4: Thou hast mitigated all thy wrath: thou hast turned away from the wrath of thine indignation.
84.5: Convert us, o God our Saviour: and avert thy wrath from us.
84.6: Wilt thou be wrath with us for ever? Or wilt thou extend thy wrath from generation unto generation?
84.7: O God thou being turned shalt quicken us: and thy people shall rejoice in thee.
84.8: Shew us, o Lord, thy mercy and give us thy salvation.
84.9: I will hear what our Lord God will speak in me: because he will speak peace upon his people. And upon his saints: and upon them, that are converted to the heart.
84.10: But yet his salvation is nigh to them that fear him: that glory may inhabit in our land.
84.11: Mercy and truth have met each other: justice and peace have kissed.
84.12: Truth is risen out of the earth: and justice hath looked down from heaven.
84.13: For our Lord certes will give benignity: and our land shall give her fruit.
84.14: Justice shall walk before him: and shall set his steps in the way.

85.1: Aprayer to David himself. Incline thine ear, o Lord, & hear me: because I am needy and poor.
85.2: Keep my soul, because I am holy: save thy servant my God, that hopeth in thee.
85.3: Have mercy on me, o Lord, because I have cried to thee all the day:
85.4: make joyful the soul of thy servant, because to thee, o Lord, have I lifted up my soul.
85.5: Because thou, o Lord, art sweet, and mild: and of much mercy to all that invocate thee.
85.6: Receive, o Lord, my prayer with thine ears: and attend to the voice of my petition.
85.7: In the day of my tribulation I have called to thee: because thou hast heard me.
85.8: There is not the like to thee amongst gods, o Lord: and there is not according to thy works.
85.9: All nations whatsoever thou hast made, shall come, and shall adore before thee, o Lord: and they shall glorify thy name.
85.10: Because thou art great and doing marvelous things: thou only art God.
85.11: Conduct me, o Lord, in thy way, and I will walk in thy truth: let my heart rejoice that it may fear thy name.
85.12: I will confess to thee, o Lord my God, with all my heart, and will glorify thy name for ever.
85.13: Because thy mercy is great upon me: & thou hast delivered my soul out of the lower hell.
85.14: O God the wicked are risen up upon me, and the synagogue of the mighty have sought my soul: and they have not set thee in their sight.
85.15: And thou, o Lord, the God of compassion and merciful, patient, and of much mercy, and true.
85.16: Have respect to me and have mercy on me, give thine empire to thy servant: and save the son of thy handmaid.
85.17: Make with me a sign unto God, that they may see which hate me, and may be confounded: because thou o Lord hast holpen me.

86.1: To the children of Core, a Psalm of Canticle. The foundations thereof in the holy mountains:
86.2: our Lord loveth the gates of Sion above all the Tabernacles of Jacob.
86.3: Glorious things are said of thee, o city of God.
86.4: I will be mindful of Rahab, and Babylon knowing me. Behold the foreigners, and Tyre, & the people of the Æthiopians, these were there.
86.5: Shall it not be said of Sion: Man and man, is born in her; and the Highest himself founded her?
86.6: Our Lord will declare in scriptures of peoples, and of Princes: of those that have been in her.
86.7: The habitation in thee, is as it were of all rejoicing.

87.1: A Canticle of a Psalm, to the children of Core, unto the end, for Maheleth to answer, of understanding to Eman the Ezrahite.
87.2: O Lord the God of my salvation: in the day have I cried, and in the night before thee.
87.3: Let my prayer enter in thy sight: incline thine ear to my petition.
87.4: Because my soul is replenished with evils: & my life hath approached to hell.
87.5: I am accounted with them that descend into the lake. I am become as a man without help,
87.6: free among the dead, as the wounded sleeping in the sepulchres, of whom thou art mindful no more: and they are cast off from thy hand.
87.7: They have put me in the lower lake: in the dark places, and in the shadow of death.
87.8: Thy fury is confirmed upon me: and all thy waves thou hast brought in upon me.
87.9: Thou hast made my familiars far from me: they have put me abomination to themselves. I was delivered and came not forth:
87.10: mine eyes languished for poverty. I cried to thee o Lord all the day: I stretched out my hands to thee.
87.11: Wilt thou do marvels to the dead: or shall physicians raise to life, and they confess to thee?
87.12: Shall any in the sepulchre declare thy mercy, and thy truth in perdition?
87.13: Shall thy marvelous works be known in darkness: and thy justice in the land of oblivion.
87.14: And I o Lord have cried to thee: and in the morning shall my prayer prevent thee.
87.15: Why doest thou, o Lord, reject my prayer: turnest away thy face from me?
87.16: I am poor, and in labours from my youth: and being exalted, humbled, and troubled.
87.17: Thy wraths have passed upon me: and thy terrors have troubled me.
87.18: They have compassed me as water all the day: they compassed me together.
87.19: Thou hast made friend, and neighbour far from me and my familiars because of misery.

88.1: Of understanding to Ethan the Ezrahite.
88.2: The mercies of our Lord I will sing for ever. In generation and generation I will shew forth thy truth in my mouth.
88.3: Because thou saidst: Mercy shall be built up for ever in the heavens: thy truth shall be prepared in them.
88.4: I have ordained a testament with mine elect, I have sworn to David my servant:
88.5: for ever will I prepare thy seed. And I will build thy seat unto generation and generation.
88.6: The heavens shall confess thy marvelous works o Lord: yea and thy truth in the Church of Saints.
88.7: For who in the clouds shall be equal to our Lord: shall be like to God among the sons of God?
88.8: God who is glorified in the counsel of Saints, great and terrible over all that are round about him.
88.9: O Lord God of hosts, who is like to thee? Thou art mighty, o Lord, and thy truth round about thee.
88.10: Thou rulest over the power of the sea: and the moving of the waves thereof thou doest mitigate.
88.11: Thou humbledst the proud one, as one wounded: in the arm of thy strength thou hast dispersed thine enemies.
88.12: The heavens are thine, and the earth is thine, the round earth, and the fullness thereof thou hast founded:
88.13: the north and the sea thou hast created. Thabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy name:
88.14: thy arm is with might. Let thy hand be confirmed, and thy right hand exalted:
88.15: justice and judgment is the preparation of thy seat. Mercy and truth shall go before thy face:
88.16: blessed is the people that knoweth jubilation. Lord they shall walk in the light of thy countenance,
88.17: and in thy name they shall rejoice all the day: and in thy justice they shall be exalted.
88.18: Because thou art the glory of their strength: and in thy good pleasure shall our horn be exalted.
88.19: Because our protection is of our Lord: and of the holy one of Israel our King.
88.20: Then didst thou speak in vision to thy Saints, and saidst: I have put help on the mighty one: & have exalted an elect one of my people.
88.21: I have found David my servant: with mine holy oil have I anointed him.
88.22: For mine hand shall help him: and mine arm shall strengthen him.
88.23: The enemy shall nothing prevail in him: and the son of iniquity shall not add to hurt him.
88.24: And I will cut down his enemies before his face: and them that hate him I will put to flight.
88.25: And my truth, and my mercy with him: and in my name shall his horn be exalted.
88.26: And I will put his hand in the sea: and his right hand in the rivers.
88.27: He shall invocate me: Thou art my Father: my God, and the protector of my Salvation.
88.28: And I will put him the first-begotten, high above the kings of the earth.
88.29: I will keep my mercy unto him for ever: and my testament faithful to him.
88.30: I will put his seed for ever and ever: and his throne as the days of heaven.
88.31: But if his children shall forsake my law: and will not walk in my judgments.
88.32: If they shall profane my justices: and not keep my commandments:
88.33: I will visit their iniquities with a rod: and their sins with stripes.
88.34: But my mercy I will not take away from him: neither will I hurt in my truth.
88.35: Neither will I profane my testament: and the words that proceed from my mouth I will not make frustrate.
88.36: Once I have sworn in my holy, if I lie to David:
88.37: his seed shall continue for ever.
88.38: And his throne as the Sun in my sight, and as the Moon perfect for ever: and a faithful witness in heaven.
88.39: But thou hast repelled and despised: thou hast deferred thy Christ.
88.40: Thou hast overthrown the testament of thy servant: thou hast profaned his sanctuary on the earth.
88.41: Thou hast destroyed all the hedges thereof: thou hast made the firmament thereof fear.
88.42: All that pass by the way have spoiled him: he is become a reproach to his neighbours.
88.43: Thou hast exalted the right hand of them that oppress him: thou hast made all his enemies joyful.
88.44: Thou hast turned away the help of his sword: and hast not holpen him in battle.
88.45: Thou hast destroyed him from emundation: and his seat thou hast broken down to the ground.
88.46: Thou hast lessened the days of his time: thou hast overwhelmed him with confusion.
88.47: How long, o Lord, doest thou turn away for ever: shall thy wrath burn as a fire?
88.48: Remember what my substance is: for hast thou made all the children of men in vain?
88.49: Who is the man that shall live, and shall not see death: shall deliver his soul from the hand of hell?
88.50: Where are thine old mercies, o Lord, as thou swarest to David in thy truth?
88.51: Be mindful, o Lord, of the reproach of thy servants (which I have held in my bosom) of many nations.
88.52: Which thine enemies have reproached, o Lord, which they have reproached the commutation of thy Christ.
88.53: Blessed be our Lord for ever: Be it, be it.

89.1: Aprayer of Moyses the man of God. Lord, thou art made a refuge for us: from generation unto generation.
89.2: Before the mountains were made, or the earth and the world formed, from everlasting even unto everlasting thou art God.
89.3: Turn not away man into humiliation: thou saidst: Be converted ye children of men.
89.4: Because a thousand years before thine eyes, are as yesterday that is past. And as a watch in the night,
89.5: things that are counted nothing shall their years be.
89.6: In the morning as an herb he shall pass, in the morning he shall flourish, and pass: in the evening he shall fall, be hardened, and withered.
89.7: Because we have fainted in thy wrath, and in thy fury we are troubled.
89.8: Thou hast put our iniquities in thy sight: our age in the light of thy countenance.
89.9: Because all our days have failed, and in thy wrath we have failed. Our years shall be considered as a spider:
89.10: the days of our years in them are seventy years. And if in strong ones eighty years: and the more of them, labour & sorrow. Because mildness is come upon us: and we shall be chastised.
89.11: Who knoweth the power of thy wrath: and for fear
89.12: to number thy wrath? So make thy right hand known: and men learned in heart, in wisdom.
89.13: Turn, o Lord, how long? And be entreated for thy servants.
89.14: We are replenished in the morning with thy mercy: and we have rejoiced, and art delighted all our days.
89.15: We have rejoiced for the days wherein thou hast humbled us: the years, wherein we have seen evils.
89.16: Look upon thy servants, and upon thy works: and direct their children.
89.17: And let the brightness of our Lord God be upon us, and direct thou the works of our hands over us: and the work of our hands do thou direct.

90.1: Praise of a Canticle to David. He that dwelleth in the help of the Highest, shall abide in the protection of the God of heaven.
90.2: He shall say to our Lord: Thou art my protector, and my refuge: my God I will hope in him.
90.3: Because he hath delivered me from the snare of the hunters, and from the sharp word.
90.4: With his shoulders shall he overshadow thee: and under his wings thou shalt hope.
90.5: With shield shall his truth compass thee: thou shalt not be afraid of the fear in the night.
90.6: Of the arrow flying in the day, of business walking in darkness: of invasion, and the midday devil.
90.7: A thousand shall fall on thy side, & ten thousand on thy right hand: but to thee it shall not approach.
90.8: But thou shalt consider with thine eyes: and shalt see the retribution of sinners.
90.9: Because thou o Lord art my hope: thou hast made the Highest thy refuge.
90.10: There shall no evil come to thee: and scourge shall not approach to thy tabernacle.
90.11: Because he hath given his Angels charge of thee: that they keep thee in all thy ways.
90.12: In their hands they shall bear thee: lest perhaps thou knock thy foot against a stone.
90.13: Upon the asp and the basiliscus thou shall walk: & thou shalt tread upon the lion, and the dragon.
90.14: Because he hath hoped in me, I will deliver him: I will protect him, because he hath known my name.
90.15: He shall cry to me, and I will hear him: with him I am in tribulation: I will deliver him, and will glorify him.
90.16: With length of days I will replenish him: and I will shew him my salvation.

91.1: A Psalm of Canticle, in the sabbath day.
91.2: It is good to confess to our Lord: and to sing to thy name, o Highest.
91.3: To shew forth thy mercy in the morning: and thy truth in the night.
91.4: In the instrument of ten strings, in Psalter: with Canticle, on the Harp.
91.5: Because thou hast delighted me, o Lord, in thy workmanship: and in the works of thy hands I will rejoice.
91.6: How are thy works magnified, o Lord! Thy cogitations are made very profound.
91.7: The unwise man will not know; and the fool will not understand these things.
91.8: When sinners shall spring up as grass: and all that work iniquity shall appear. That they may perish for ever:
91.9: but thou the Highest for ever, o Lord.
91.10: Because lo thine enemies, o Lord, because lo thine enemies shall perish: and all that work iniquity shall be dispersed.
91.11: And my horn shall be exalted, as the unicorn's: and my old age in plentiful mercy.
91.12: And mine eye hath looked upon mine enemies: and the malignant rising up against me, mine ears shall hear.
91.13: The just shall flourish as a palm tree: as the cedar of Libanus shall he be multiplied.
91.14: They that are planted in the house of our Lord, shall flourish in the courts of the house of our God.
91.15: As yet shall they be multiplied in plentiful old age: and they shall be well affected,
91.16: that they may shew forth: That the Lord our God is righteous, and there is no iniquity in him.

92.1: Praise of Canticle to David himself in the day before the sabbath, when the earth was founded. Our Lord hath reigned, he hath put on beauty: our Lord hath put on strength, and hath girded himself. For he hath established the round world, which shall not be moved.
92.2: Thy seat is prepared from that time: thou art from everlasting.
92.3: The rivers o Lord have lifted up: the rivers have lifted up their voice. The rivers have lifted up their waves,
92.4: above the voices of many waters. The surges of the sea are marvelous, marvelous is our Lord on high.
92.5: Thy testimonies are made credible exceedingly: holiness becometh thy house, o Lord for length of days.

93.1: To David himself, in the fourth of the sabbath. Our Lord God of revenges: the God of revenges hath done freely.
93.2: Be exalted thou that judgest the earth: render retribution to the proud.
93.3: How long shall sinners, o Lord: how long shall sinners glory?
93.4: Shall they utter, and speak iniquity? Shall all they speak that work injustice?
93.5: Thy people, o Lord, they have humbled: and thine inheritance they have vexed.
93.6: The widow, and the stranger they have slain: and the pupils they have killed.
93.7: And they have said: The Lord shall not see, neither shall the God of Jacob understand.
93.8: Understand ye foolish in the people: and ye fools be wise at sometime.
93.9: He that planted the ear, shall he not hear? Or he that made the eye doth he not consider?
93.10: He that chastiseth nations, shall he not rebuke: he that teacheth man knowledge?
93.11: Our Lord knoweth the cogitations of men: that they be vain.
93.12: Blessed is the man whom thou shalt instruct, o Lord, and shalt teach out of thy law.
93.13: That thou mayst give him quietness from the evil days: till a pit be digged for the sinner.
93.14: Because our Lord will not reject his people: and his inheritance he will not forsake.
93.15: Until justice be turned into judgment: and they who are near it, are all that are right of heart.
93.16: Who shall rise for me against the malignant? Or who shall stand with me against them that work iniquity?
93.17: But that our Lord hath holpen me: within very little my soul had dwelt in hell.
93.18: If I said: My foot is moved: thy mercy, o Lord, did help me.
93.19: According to the multitude of my sorrows in my heart: thy consolations have made my soul joyful.
93.20: Doth the seat of iniquity cleave to thee: which makest labour in precept?
93.21: They will hunt after the soul of the just: and will condemn innocent blood.
93.22: And our Lord became my refuge: and my God the help of my hope.
93.23: And he will repay them their iniquity: and in their malice he will destroy them: the Lord our God will destroy them.

94.1: Praise of Canticle, to David himself. Come, let us rejoice to our Lord: let us make jubilation to God our saviour.
94.2: Let us prevent his face in confession: and in Psalms let us make jubilation to him.
94.3: Because our Lord is a great God: and a great King above all gods.
94.4: Because in his hand are the ends of the earth: and the heights of the mountains be his.
94.5: Because the sea is his, and he made it: and his hands formed the dry land.
94.6: Come let us adore, and fall down: and weep before our Lord, that made us.
94.7: Because he is the Lord our God, and we the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand.
94.8: Today if ye shall hear his voice, harden not your hearts.
94.9: As in the provocation according to the day of the tentation in the desert: where your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works.
94.10: Forty years was I offended with that generation, and said: These always err in heart.
94.11: And these have not known my ways: as I sware in my wrath: if they shall enter into my rest.

95.1: A Canticle to David himself, when the house was built after the captivity. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: sing to our Lord all the earth.
