95.2: Sing ye to our Lord, and bless his name: shew forth his salvation from day to day.
95.3: Shew forth his glory among the Gentiles, his marvelous works in all peoples.
95.4: Because our Lord is great, and exceeding laudable: he is terrible above all gods.
95.5: Because all the gods of the Gentiles are devils, but our Lord made the heavens.
95.6: Confession and beauty in his sight: holiness and magnificence in his sanctification.
95.7: Bring to our Lord ye families of Gentiles, bring ye to our Lord glory and honour:
95.8: bring to our Lord glory unto his name. Take up hosts, and enter into his courts:
95.9: adore ye our Lord in his holy court. Let all the earth be moved before his face:
95.10: Say ye among the Gentiles that our Lord hath reigned. For he hath corrected the round world, which shall not be moved: he will judge peoples in equity.
95.11: Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice, the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof:
95.12: the fields shall be glad, and all things that are in them. Then shall the trees of the woods rejoice
95.13: before the face of our Lord, because he cometh: because he cometh to judge the earth. He will judge the round world in equity, and peoples in his truth.

96.1: To this David, when his land was restored again to him. Our Lord hath reigned, let the earth rejoice: let many Islands be glad.
96.2: Cloud and mist round about him: justice and judgment the correction of his seat.
96.3: Fire shall go before him, and shall inflame his enemies round about.
96.4: His lightnings shined to the round world, the earth saw: and was moved.
96.5: The mountains melted as wax, before the face of our Lord: before the face of our Lord all the earth.
96.6: The heavens have shewed forth his justice: and all peoples have seen his glory.
96.7: Let them all be confounded, that adore sculptiles: and that glory in their idols. Adore him all ye his Angels:
96.8: Sion heard, and was glad. And the daughters of Juda rejoiced, because of thy judgments, o Lord.
96.9: Because thou Lord most high over all the earth: thou art exalted exceedingly above all gods.
96.10: You that love our Lord, hate ye evil, our Lord keepeth the souls of his saints, out of the hand of the sinner he will deliver them.
96.11: Light is risen to the just, and joy to the right of heart.
96.12: Be glad ye just in our Lord: and confess ye to the memory of his sanctification.

97.1: A Psalm to David himself. Sing ye to our Lord a new song: because he hath done marvelous things. His right hand hath wrought salvation to himself: and his arm is holy.
97.2: Our Lord hath made known his salvation: in the sight of the Gentiles he hath revealed his justice.
97.3: He hath remembered his mercy, and his truth to the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
97.4: Make ye jubilation to God all the earth: chant, and rejoice, and sing.
97.5: Sing to our Lord on harp, on harp and voice of Psalm:
97.6: on long drawn trumpets, and voice of cornet of horn. Make jubilation in the sight of the King our Lord:
97.7: let the sea be moved, and the fullness thereof: the round world, and they that dwell therein.
97.8: The rivers shall clap with hand: the mountains together shall rejoice
97.9: at the sight of our Lord: because he cometh to judge the earth. He will judge the round earth in justice, and the peoples in equity.

98.1: Apsalm to David himself. Our Lord hath reigned, let peoples be angry: he that sitteth upon the Cherubs, let the earth be moved.
98.2: Our Lord great in Sion: and high above all peoples.
98.3: Let them confess to thy great name: because it is terrible and holy.
98.4: And the honour of the King loveth judgment. Thou hast prepared directions: thou hast done judgment and justice in Jacob.
98.5: Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore his foot-stool: because it is holy.
98.6: Moyses and Aaron in his Priests: and Samuel among them that invocate his name: They invocated our Lord and he heard them:
98.7: in a pillar of a cloud he spake to them. They kept his testimonies, & the precept which he gave them.
98.8: O Lord our God thou heardest them: God thou wast propitious to them, and taking vengeance upon all their inventions.
98.9: Exalt ye the Lord our God, and adore ye in his holy mount: because the Lord our God is holy.

99.2: Make ye jubilation to God all the earth: serve ye our Lord in gladness. Enter ye in before his sight, in exultation.
99.3: Know ye that our Lord he is God: he made us, & not we ourselves. His people, and the sheep of his pasture:
99.4: enter ye into his gates in confession, his courts in hymns, confess ye to him. Praise ye his name:
99.5: because our Lord is sweet, his mercy for ever, and his truth even unto generation and generation.

100.1: A Psalm to David himself. Mercy and judgment I will sing to thee, o Lord: I will sing,
100.2: and I shall understand in the immaculate way, when thou shalt come to me. I walked through in the innocency of my heart, in the midst of my house.
100.3: I did not propose before mine eyes any unjust thing: I hated them that do prevarication.
100.4: Aperverse heart hath not cleaved to me: the malignant declining from me I knew not.
100.5: One secretly detracting from his neighbour, him did I persecute. One of a proud eye, and unsatiable heart, with him I did not eat.
100.6: Mine eyes are towards the faithful of the earth that they may sit with me. A man that walketh in the immaculate way he did minister to me.
100.7: He that doth proudly shall not dwell in the midst of my house: he that speaketh unjust things, hath not directed in the sight of mine eyes.
100.8: In the morning did I kill all the sinners of the earth: that I might destroy out of the city of our Lord all those that work iniquity.

101.1: The prayer of the poor, when he shall be anxious, and shall make his petition before our Lord.
101.2: Lord hear my prayer: and let my cry come to thee.
101.3: Turn not away thy face from me: in what day soever I am in tribulation, incline thine ear to me. In what day soever I shall invocate thee, hear me speedily.
101.4: Because my days have vanished as smoke: and my bones are withered as a dry burnt firebrand.
101.5: I am strucken as grass, and my heart is withered: because I have forgotten to eat my bread.
101.6: For the voice of my groaning, my bone hath cleaved to my flesh.
101.7: I am become like a pelican of the wilderness: I am become as a night crow in the house.
101.8: I have watched, and am become as a sparrow solitary in the house-top.
101.9: All the day did mine enemies upbraid me: and they that praised me sware against me.
101.10: Because I did eat ashes as bread, & mingled my drink with weeping.
101.11: At the face of thy wrath and indignation: because lifting me up thou hast thrown me down.
101.12: My days have declined as a shadow: and I am withered as grass.
101.13: But thou o Lord endurest for ever: and thy memorial in generation and generation.
101.14: Thou rising up shalt have mercy on Sion: because it is time to have mercy on it, because the time cometh.
101.15: Because the stones thereof have pleased thy servants: and they shall have pity on the earth thereof.
101.16: And the Gentiles shall fear thy name o Lord, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.
101.17: Because our Lord hath built Sion: and he shall be seen in his glory.
101.18: He hath had respect to the prayer of the humble: and he hath not despised their petition.
101.19: Let these things be written unto another generation: and the people that shall be created shall praise our Lord.
101.20: Because he hath looked forth from his high holy place: our Lord from heaven hath looked upon the earth.
101.21: That he might hear the groanings of the fettered: that he might loose the children of them that are slain:
101.22: That they may shew forth the name of our Lord in Sion: and his praise in Jerusalem.
101.23: In the assembling of the people together in one, and kings to serve our Lord.
101.24: He answered him in the way of his strength: Shew me the fewness of my days.
101.25: Call me not back in the half of my days: thy years are unto generation and generation.
101.26: In the beginning, o Lord, thou didst found the earth: and the heavens are the works of thy hands.
101.27: They shall perish, but thou art permanent: and they shall all wax old as a garment. And as a vesture thou shalt change them, and they shall be changed:
101.28: but thou art the self-same, and thy years shall not fail.
101.29: The children of thy servants shall inhabit: and their seed shall be directed for ever.

102.1: To David himself. My soul bless thou our Lord: and all things, that are within me, his holy name.
102.2: My soul bless thou our Lord: and forget not all his retributions.
102.3: Who is propitious to all thine iniquities: who healeth all thine infirmities.
102.4: Who redeemeth thy life from deadly falling: who crowneth thee in mercy and commiserations.
102.5: Who replenisheth thy desire in good things: thy youth shall be renewed as the eagle's.
102.6: Our Lord doth mercies: and judgment to all that suffer wrong.
102.7: He made his ways known to Moyses, his wills to the children of Israel.
102.8: Our Lord is pitiful, and merciful: long suffering, and very merciful.
102.9: He will not be angry always: neither will he threaten for ever.
102.10: He hath not done to us according to our sins: neither according to our iniquities hath he rewarded us.
102.11: For according to the height of heaven from the earth: hath he strengthened his mercy upon them that fear him.
102.12: As far as the East is distant from the West: hath he made our iniquities far from us.
102.13: As a father hath compassion of his children, so hath our Lord compassion on them that fear him:
102.14: because he hath known our making. He remembered that we are dust:
102.15: man, his days are as grass, as the flower of the field, so shall he flourish.
102.16: Because the spirit shall pass in him, and he shall not stand: and he shall know his place no more.
102.17: But the mercy of our Lord from everlasting, and unto everlasting upon them that fear him. And his justice is upon the children's children, to them that keep his testament.
102.18: And are mindful of his commandments to do them.
102.19: Our Lord hath prepared his seat in heaven: and his Kingdom shall have dominion over all.
102.20: Bless our Lord all ye his Angels: mighty in power, doing his word, that fear the voice of his words.
102.21: Bless our Lord all ye his hosts: you his ministers, that do his will.
102.22: Bless ye our Lord all his works: in every place of his dominion, my soul bless thou our Lord.

103.1: To David himself. My soul bless thou our Lord: o Lord my God thou art magnified exceedingly.
103.2: Thou hast put on confession and beauty: being clothed with light as with a garment. Stretching out the heaven as a skin:
103.3: which coverest the higher parts thereof with waters. Which makest the cloud for thee to ascend on: which walkest upon the wings of winds.
103.4: Which makest spirits thine Angels: and thy ministers a burning fire.
103.5: Which hast founded the earth upon the stability thereof: it shall not be inclined for ever and ever.
103.6: The depth, as a garment, is his clothing: upon the mountains shall waters stand.
103.7: At thy reprehension they shall fly: at the voice of thy thunder they shall fear.
103.8: The mountains ascend: and the plain fields descend into the place, which thou hast founded for them.
103.9: Thou hast set a bound, which they shall not pass over: neither shall they return to cover the earth.
103.10: Which sendest forth fountains in the valleys: between the midst of mountains shall waters pass.
103.11: All the beasts of the field shall drink: the wild asses shall expect in their thirst.
103.12: Over them shall the fowls of the air inhabit: out of the midst of rocks they shall give forth voices.
103.13: Watering the mountains from higher places: of the fruit of thy work shall the earth be filled.
103.14: Bringing forth grass for beasts, and herb for the service of men. That thou mayst bring forth bread out of the earth:
103.15: & wine may make the heart of man joyful. That he may make the face cheerful with oil: and bread may confirm the heart of man.
103.16: The trees of the field shall be filled, and the cedars of Libanus, which he hath planted:
103.17: there sparrows shall make their nest. The house of the heron is the leader of them:
103.18: the high mountains for harts: the rock a refuge for the Irchins.
103.19: He made the moon for seasons: the sun knoweth his going down.
103.20: Thou didst appoint darkness, and night was made: in it shall all the beasts of the wood pass.
103.21: The whelps of lions roaring, to raven, and to seek of God meat for themselves.
103.22: The sun is risen, & they are gathered together: and in their couches they shall be placed.
103.23: Man shall go forth to his work: and to his working until evening.
103.24: How magnified are thy works, o Lord! Thou hast made all things in wisdom: the earth is filled with thy possession.
103.25: This great sea, and very large, there are creeping beasts, whereof there is no number. Little beasts with great:
103.26: there ships shall pass. This dragon, whom thou madest to delude:
103.27: all expect of thee that thou give them meat in season.
103.28: Thou giving unto them, they shall gather it: thou opening thy hand, all shall be filled with bounty.
103.29: But thou turning away the face, they shall be troubled: thou shalt take away their spirit, and they shall fail, and shall return into their dust.
103.30: Thou shalt send forth thy spirit, and they shall be created: and thou shalt renew the face of the earth.
103.31: Be the glory of our Lord for ever: our Lord will rejoice in his works.
103.32: Who looketh upon the earth, & maketh it to tremble: who toucheth the mountains, and they smoke.
103.33: I will chant to our Lord in my life: I will sing to my God as long as I am.
103.34: Let my speech be acceptable to him: but I will take delight in our Lord.
103.35: Let sinners fail from the earth, and the unjust, so that they be not: my soul bless thou our Lord.

104.1: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord, and invocate his name: shew forth his works among the Gentiles.
104.2: Chant to him, and sing to him: tell ye all his marvelous works.
104.3: Praise ye him in his holy name: let the heart of them rejoice that seek our Lord.
104.4: Seek ye our Lord, and be confirmed: seek his face always.
104.5: Remember ye his marvelous works, which he hath done: his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth.
104.6: The seed of Abraham his servants: the children of Jacob his elect.
104.7: He is the Lord our God: in all the earth are his judgments.
104.8: He hath been mindful for ever of his testament; of the word which he commanded unto a thousand generations.
104.9: Which he disposed to Abraham: and of his oath to Isaac.
104.10: And he appointed it to Jacob for a precept: and to Israel for an eternal testament,
104.11: saying: To thee will I give the land of Chanaan, the cord of your inheritance.
104.12: When they were of small number, very few and sojourners thereof:
104.13: And they passed from nation into nation, & from Kingdom to another people.
104.14: He left not a man to hurt them: and he rebuked kings for their sake:
104.15: Touch not my anointed, and toward my Prophets be not malignant.
104.16: And he called a famine upon the land: and he destroyed all the strength of bread.
104.17: He sent a man before them: Joseph was sold to be a servant.
104.18: They humbled his feet in fetters, iron passed through his soul,
104.19: until his word came. The word of our Lord inflamed him:
104.20: the king sent, and loosed him; the Prince of the people, and released him.
104.21: He appointed him lord of his house: and Prince of all his possession.
104.22: That he might instruct his Princes as himself: and might teach his ancients wisdom.
104.23: And Israel entered into Ægypt, and Jacob was a sojourner in the land of Cham.
104.24: And he increased his people exceedingly: and strengthened them over their enemies.
104.25: He turned their heart that they hated his people: and to work guile toward his servants.
104.26: He sent Moyses his servant: Aaron himself whom he chose.
104.27: He did put in them the words of his signs, and of his wonders in the Land of Cham.
104.28: He sent darkness, and obscured: and did not exasperate his words.
104.29: He turned their waters into blood: and killed their fishes.
104.30: Their land brought forth frogs in the inner chambers of their kings.
104.31: He said, and the cænomyia came: and the sciniphs in all their coasts.
104.32: He made their rains hail: fire burning in their land.
104.33: And he struck their vines, and their fig-trees: and he destroyed the wood of their coasts.
104.34: He said, & the locust came, & the bruchus whereof there was no number.
104.35: And it did eat all the grass in their land: and it did eat all the fruit of their land.
104.36: And he struck every first begotten in their land: the first fruits of all their labour.
104.37: And he brought them forth with gold and silver, and there was not in their tribes a feeble person.
104.38: Ægypt was glad at their departure: because the fear of them lay upon them.
104.39: He spread a cloud for their protection, and fire to shine unto them by night.
104.40: They made petition, and the quail came: and he filled them with the bread of heaven.
104.41: He divided the rock, and waters flowed: rivers ran in the dry ground.
104.42: Because he was mindful of his holy word, which he had uttered to Abraham his servant.
104.43: And he brought forth his people in exultation, and his elect in joy.
104.44: And he gave them the countries of the Nations: and they possessed the labours of peoples:
104.45: That they might keep his justifications, and seek after his law.

105.1: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
105.2: Who shalt speak the powers of our Lord, shall make all his praises to be heard?
105.3: Blessed are they, that keep judgment, & do justice at all time.
105.4: Remember us, o Lord in the good pleasure of thy people: visit us in thy salvation:
105.5: To see in the goodness of thine elect, to rejoice in the joy of thy nation: that thou mayst be praised with thine inheritance.
105.6: We have sinned with our fathers: we have dealt unjustly, we have done iniquity.
105.7: Our fathers in Ægypt did not understand thy marvelous works: they were not mindful of the multitude of thy mercy. And they provoked thee to wrath going up unto the sea, the Red sea.
105.8: And he saved them for his name sake; that he might make his power known.
105.9: And he rebuked the Red sea, and it was made dry: and he led them in the depths as in a desert.
105.10: And he saved them from the hand of them that hated them: and he redeemed them out of the hand of the enemy.
105.11: And water overwhelmed those that afflicted them: there did not one of them remain.
105.12: And they believed his words: and they sang his praises.
105.13: They had quickly done, they forgot his works: and they expected not his counsel.
105.14: And they coveted concupiscence in the desert: and tempted God in the place without water.
105.15: He gave them their petition: and sent saturity into their souls.
105.16: And they provoked Moyses in the camp: Aaron the holy of our Lord.
105.17: The earth was opened, and swallowed Dathan, and overwhelmed the congregation of Abiron.
105.18: And a fire flamed up in their synagogue: the flame burnt the sinners.
105.19: And they made a calf in Horeb: and they adored the sculptile.
105.20: And they changed their glory into the similitude of a calf that eateth grass.
105.21: They forgat God, which saved them, which did great things in Ægypt,
105.22: marvelous things in the land of Cham, terrible things in the Red sea.
105.23: And he said to destroy them: if Moyses his elect had not stood in the way before him . To turn away his wrath that he should not destroy them:
105.24: and they esteemed for naught the land that was to be desired. They did not believe his word.
105.25: and they murmured in their tabernacles: they heard not the voice of our Lord.
105.26: And he lifted up his hand over them: to overthrow them in the desert:
105.27: And to cast down their seed among the Nations: & to disperse them in the countries.
105.28: And they were professed to Beelphegor: and they did eat the sacrifices of the dead.
105.29: And they provoked him in their inventions: & ruin was multiplied on them.
105.30: And Phinees stood, and pacified: and the slaughter ceased.
105.31: And it was reputed to him unto justice, in generation and generation even for ever.
105.32: And they provoked him at the waters of contradiction: and Moyses was vexed for them:
105.33: because they exasperated his spirit. And he plainly affirmed in his lips:
105.34: they destroyed not the nations, of which our Lord spake to them.
105.35: And they were mingled among the nations, and learned their works:
105.36: and they served their sculptiles: and it became a scandal to them.
105.37: And they immolated their sons, and their daughters to devils.
105.38: And they shed innocent blood: the blood of their sons and of their daughters, which they sacrificed to the sculptiles of Chanaan. And the land was infected with blood,
105.39: & was contaminated in their works: and they did fornicate in their inventions.
105.40: And our Lord was wrath with fury upon his people: and he abhorred his inheritance.
105.41: And he delivered them into the hands of the nations: and they that hated them had the dominion of them.
105.42: And their enemies afflicted them, and they were humbled under their hands:
105.43: he did often deliver them. But they exasperated him in their counsel: and they were humbled in their iniquities.
105.44: And he saw when they were afflicted: and he heard their prayer.
105.45: And he was mindful of his testament: and it repented him according to the multitude of his mercy.
105.46: And he gave them into mercies in the sight of all, that had taken them.
105.47: Save us, o Lord our God: and gather us out of the Nations. That we may confess to thy holy name: & may glory in thy praise.
105.48: Blessed be our Lord the God of Israel from everlasting unto everlasting: and all the people shall say: Be it, be it.

106.1: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord, because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
106.2: Let them say that are redeemed of our Lord, whom he redeemed out of the hand of the enemy: and out of the countries he gathered them:
106.3: From the rising of the sun, and the going down: from the north, and the sea.
106.4: They wandered in the wilderness, in a place without water: the way of city for habitation they found not.
106.5: Hungry and thirsty: their soul fainted in them.
106.6: And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
106.7: And he conducted them into the right way: to go into a city of habitation.
106.8: Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: and his marvelous works to the children of men.
106.9: Because he hath filled the empty soul: and the hungry soul he hath filled with good things.
106.10: Them that sat in darkness, and in the shadow of death: bound in neediness, and iron.
106.11: Because they exasperated the words of God: and they provoked the counsel of the Highest.
106.12: And their heart was humbled in labours: they were weakened, neither was there any to help.
106.13: And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
106.14: And he brought them out of darkness, and the shadow of death: and brake their bonds asunder.
106.15: Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: & his marvelous works to the children of men.
106.16: Because he hath destroyed the gates of brass; and the bars of iron he hath broken.
106.17: He hath received them out of the way of their iniquity: for they were humbled for their injustices.
106.18: Their soul did abhor all meat: & they approached even to the gates of death.
106.19: And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation: and he delivered them out of their necessities.
106.20: He sent his word, and healed them: and delivered them out of their destructions.
106.21: Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: & his marvelous works to the children of men.
106.22: And let them sacrifice the sacrifice of praise: & shew forth his works in exultation.
106.23: They that go down into the sea in ships, making traffic in the great waters.
106.24: They have seen the works of our Lord, and his marvelous things in the depth.
106.25: He said, and the blast of the storm stood: and the waves thereof were exalted.
106.26: They ascend even to the heavens, and they descend even to the depths: their soul pined away in evils.
106.27: They were troubled, and were moved as a drunken man: and all their wisdom was devoured.
106.28: And they cried to our Lord when they were in tribulation, and he brought them out of their necessities.
106.29: And he turned his storm into calm: & the waves thereof were quiet.
106.30: And they rejoiced because they were quiet: and he conducted them into the haven of their will.
106.31: Let the mercies of our Lord confess to him: and his marvelous works to the children of men.
106.32: And let them exalt him in the Church of the people: and the chair of the ancients let them praise him.
106.33: He turned the rivers into a desert: and the issues of waters into dryness.
106.34: The fruitful land into a salt ground, for the malice of them that inhabit it.
106.35: He turned the desert into pools of waters: and the land without water into issues of waters.
106.36: And he placed the hungry there, and they built a city of habitation.
106.37: And they sowed fields and planted vineyards: and they made fruit of nativity.
106.38: And he blessed them, and they were multiplied exceedingly: and their beasts he lessened not.
106.39: And they were made few: and were vexed by the tribulation of evils, and with sorrow.
106.40: Contempt was poured out upon Princes: and he made them wander where was no way, and not in the way.
106.41: And he did help the poor out of poverty: and made families as sheep.
106.42: The just shall see, and shall rejoice: & all iniquity shall stop her mouth.
106.43: Who is wise and will keep these things? And will understand the mercies of our Lord?

107.1: Acanticle of Psalm, to David himself.
107.2: My heart is ready, o God, my heart is ready : I will chant and will sing in my glory.
107.3: Arise my glory, arise psalter and harp: I will arise early.
107.4: I will confess to thee in peoples, o Lord: and I will sing to thee in the Nations.
107.5: Because thy mercy is great above the heavens: & thy truth even to the clouds.
107.6: Be exalted above the heavens o God, and thy glory over all the earth:
107.7: that thy beloved may be delivered. Save with thy right hand, and hear me:
107.8: God spake in his holy: I will rejoice, and will divide Sichem, and I will measure the vale of tabernacles.
107.9: Galaad is mine, and Manasses is mine: and Ephraim the protection of my head. Juda is my king:
107.10: Moab the pot of my hope. Upon Idumea I will extend my shoe: the strangers are made my friends.
107.11: Who will conduct me into a fenced city? Who will conduct me into Idumea?
107.12: Wilt not thou, o God, which hast repelled us, and wilt not thou go forth, o God, in our hosts?
107.13: Give us help out of tribulation: because man's salvation is vain.
107.14: In God we shall do strength: and he will bring our enemies to nothing.

108.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
108.2: O God conceal not my praise: because the mouth of the sinner, and the mouth of the deceitful man is open upon me.
108.3: They have spoken against me with deceitful tongue, and with words of hatred they have compassed me: and they have impugned me without cause.
108.4: For that they should love me, they backbited me: but I prayed.
108.5: And they set against me evil things for good: and hatred for my love.
108.6: Appoint a sinner over him: and let the devil stand on his right hand.
108.7: When he is judged, let him come forth condemned: and let his prayer be turned into sin.
108.8: Let his days be made few: and let another take his bishoprick.
108.9: Let his children be made orphans: and his wife a widow.
108.10: Let his children be transported wandering, and let them beg: and let them be cast out of their habitations.
108.11: Let the usurer search all his substance: and let strangers spoil his labours.
108.12: Let there be none to help him: neither let there be any to have pity on his pupils.
108.13: Let his children come to destruction: in one generation let his name be clean put out.
108.14: Let the iniquity of his fathers return to memory in the sight of our Lord: and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
108.15: Let them be before our Lord always, and let the memory of them perish out of the earth:
108.16: For that he remembered not to do mercy.
108.17: And he persecuted the poor, and needy man, and the compunct in heart to kill him.
108.18: And he loved cursing, and it shall come to him: and he would not blessing, and it shall be far from him. And he put on cursing as a garment, and it entered as water into his inner parts, and as oil in his bones.
108.19: Be it to him as a garment, wherewith he is covered: and as a girdle, wherewith he is always girded.
108.20: This is the work of them that detract from me before our Lord: and that speak evils against my soul.
108.21: And thou Lord, Lord, do with me for thy name's sake: because thy mercy is sweet. Deliver me
108.22: because I am needy and poor: and my heart is troubled within me.
108.23: As a shadow when it declineth, am I taken away: and I am shaken as locusts.
108.24: My knees are weakened with fasting: and my flesh is changed by reason of oil.
108.25: And I am made a reproach to them: they saw me, and wagged their heads.
108.26: Help me, o Lord my God: save me according to thy mercy.
108.27: And let them know that this is thy hand: & thou, o Lord, hast done it.
108.28: They will curse, and thou shalt bless: let them that rise up against me be confounded: but thy servant shall rejoice.
108.29: Let them that detract from me, be clothed with shame: and let them be covered with their confusion, as with a double patched cloak.
108.30: I will confess to our Lord exceedingly with my mouth: and in the midst of many I will praise him.
108.31: Because he hath stood on the right hand of the poor, that he might save my soul from the persecutors.

109.1: A Psalm of David. Our Lord said to my Lord: Sit on my right hand: till I make thine enemies, the foot-stool of thy feet.
109.2: Our Lord will send forth the rod of thy strength from Sion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
109.3: With thee the beginning in the day of thy strength: in the brightness of holy things: from the womb before the day star I begat thee.
109.4: Our Lord sware, and it shall not repent him: Thou art a Priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedech.
109.5: Our Lord on thy right hand hath broken kings in the day of his wrath.
109.6: He shall judge in nations, he shall fill ruins, he shall crush the heads in the land of many.
109.7: Of the torrent in the way he shall drink: therefore shall he exalt the head.

110.1: Alleluia. I will confess to thee, o Lord, with all my heart: in the counsel of the just, and the congregation.
110.2: The works of our Lord are great: exquisite according to all his wills.
110.3: Confession and magnificence his work: and his justice continueth for ever and ever.
110.4: He hath made a memory of his marvelous works; a merciful & pitiful Lord:
110.5: he hath given meat to them that fear him. He will be mindful for ever of his testament:
110.6: the force of his works he will shew forth to his people:
110.7: To give them the inheritance of the gentiles: the works of his hands truth and judgment.
110.8: All his commandments are faithful: confirmed for ever and ever, made in truth and equity.
110.9: He sent redemption to his people: he commanded his testament for ever. Holy and terrible is his name:
110.10: the fear of our Lord is the beginning of wisdom. Understanding is good to all that do it: his praise remaineth for ever and ever.

111.1: Alleluia. Of the return of Aggeus and Zachary. Blessed is the man that feareth our Lord: he shall have great delight in his commandments.
111.2: His seed shall be mighty in the earth: the generation of the righteous shall be blessed.
111.3: Glory and riches in his house: and his justice abideth for ever and ever.
111.4: Light is risen up in darkness to the righteous: he is merciful, and pitiful, and just.
111.5: Acceptable is the man, that is merciful and lendeth, that shall dispose his words in judgment:
111.6: Because he shall not be moved for ever.
111.7: The just shall be in eternal memory: he shall not fear at the hearing of evil. His heart is ready to hope in our Lord,
111.8: his heart is confirmed: he shall not be moved till he look over his enemies.
111.9: He distributed, he gave to the poor: his justice remaineth for ever and ever, his horn shall be exalted in glory.
111.10: The sinner shall see, and will be angry, he shall gnash his teeth and pine away: the desire of sinners shall perish.

112.1: Alleluia. Praise our Lord ye children: praise ye the name of our Lord.
112.2: Be the name of our Lord blessed, from henceforth now and for ever,
112.3: From the rising of the sun unto the going down, the name of our Lord is laudable.
112.4: Our Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
112.5: Who is as the Lord our God, that dwelleth on high,
112.6: and beholdeth the low things in heaven and in earth?
112.7: Raising up the needy from the earth, and lifting up the poor out of the dung:
112.8: To place him with Princes, with the Princes of his people.
112.9: Who maketh the barren woman to dwell in a house, a joyful mother of children.

113.1: Alleluia. In the coming forth of Israel out of Ægypt: of the house of Jacob from the barbarous people.
113.2: Jewry was made his sanctification, Israel his dominion.
113.3: The sea saw, and fled: Jordan was turned backward.
113.4: The mountains leaped as rams: and the little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113.5: What aileth thee o sea that thou didst fly: and thou o Jordan, that thou was turned backward?
113.6: Ye mountains leaped as rams, and ye little hills as the lambs of sheep.
113.7: At the face of our Lord was the earth moved, at the face of the God of Jacob.
113.8: Who turned the rock into pools of waters, and stony hill into fountains of waters.
113.9: Not to us O Lord, Not TO US: but to thy name give the glory.
113.10: For thy mercy, and thy truth: lest at any time the Gentiles say: Where is their God?
113.11: But our God is in heaven: he hath done all things what soever he would.
113.12: The idols of the gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
113.13: They have mouth, and shall not speak: they have eyes, an shall not see.
113.14: They have ears, and shall not hear: they have nostrils and shall not smell.
113.15: They have hands, and shall not handle: they have feet, and shall not walk: they shall not cry in their throat.
113.16: Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
113.17: The house of Israel hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113.18: The house of Aaron hath hoped in our Lord: he is their helper and their protector.
113.19: They that fear our Lord have hoped in our Lord; he is their helper and their protector.
113.20: Our Lord hath been mindful of us: and hath blessed us. He hath blessed the house of Israel: he hath blessed the house of Aaron.
113.21: He hath blessed all that fear our Lord, the little with the great.
113.22: Our Lord add upon you: upon you, & upon your children.
113.23: Blessed be you of our Lord, which made heaven and earth.
113.24: The heaven of heaven is to our Lord: but the earth he hath given to the children of men.
113.25: The dead shall not praise thee, o Lord: nor all they that go down into hell:
113.26: but we that live do bless our Lord, from this time, and for ever.

114.1: Alleluia. I have loved, because our Lord will hear the voice of my prayer.
114.2: Because he hath inclined his ear to me: and in my days I will invocate.
114.3: The sorrows of death have compassed me: and the perils of hell have found me. I have found tribulation and sorrow:
114.4: and I invocated the name of our Lord. O Lord deliver my soul:
114.5: our Lord is merciful, and just, and our God doth mercy.
114.6: Our Lord keepeth little ones: I was humbled, and he hath delivered me.
114.7: Turn o my soul into thy rest: because our Lord hath done good to thee.
114.8: Because he hath delivered my soul from death: mine eyes from tears, my feet from sliding.
114.9: I will please our Lord in the country of the living.

115.1: Alleluia. I believed, for which cause I spake: but I was humbled exceedingly.
115.2: Isaid in mine excess: Every man is a liar.
115.3: What shall I render to our Lord, for all things that he hath rendered to me?
115.4: I will take the chalice of salvation: and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115.5: I will render my vows to our Lord before all his people:
115.6: precious in the sight of our Lord is the death of his Saints.
115.7: O Lord because I am thy servant: I am thy servant, and the son of thy handmaid. Thou hast broken my bonds:
115.8: I will sacrifice to thee the host of praise, and I will invocate the name of our Lord.
115.9: I will render my vows to our Lord in the sight of all his people:
115.10: in the courts of the house of our Lord, in the midst of thee, o Jerusalem.

116.1: Alleluia. Praise our Lord all ye Gentiles: praise him all ye peoples.
116.2: Because his mercy is confirmed, upon us: and his truth remaineth for ever.

117.1: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
117.2: Let Israel now say that he is good: that his mercy is for ever.
117.3: Let the house of Aaron now say: that his mercy is for ever.
117.4: Let them now say which fear our Lord: that his mercy is for ever.
117.5: From tribulation I invocated our Lord: and our Lord heard me in largeness.
117.6: Our Lord is my helper: I will not fear what man can do to me.
117.7: Our Lord is my helper: and I will look over mine enemies.
117.8: It is good to hope in our Lord, rather than to hope in man.
117.9: It is good to hope in our Lord, rather than to hope in Princes.
117.10: All nations have compassed me: and in the name of our Lord am I revenged on them.
117.11: Compassing they have compassed me: and in the name of our Lord I was revenged on them.
117.12: They compassed me as bees, and were inflamed as fire in thorns: and in the name of our Lord I was revenged on them.
117.13: Being thrust I was overturned to fall: and our Lord received me.
117.14: Our Lord is my strength, and my praise: and he is made my salvation.
117.15: The voice of exultation, and of salvation in the tabernacles of the just.
117.16: The right hand of our Lord hath wrought strength: the right hand of our Lord hath exalted me, the right hand of our Lord hath wrought strength.
117.17: Ishall not die, but shall live: and I will tell the works of our Lord.
117.18: Our Lord chastising hath chastised me: and to death he hath not delivered me.
117.19: Open ye the gates of justice to me, being entered into them I will confess to our Lord:
117.20: this is the gate of our Lord, the just shall enter into it.
117.21: I will confess to thee because thou hast heard me: and art become my salvation.
117.22: The stone, which the builders rejected: the same is made into the head of the corner.
117.23: This was done by our Lord: and it is marvelous in our eyes.
117.24: This is the day, which our Lord made: let us rejoice, and be glad therein.
117.25: O Lord save me, o Lord give good success: Blessed be he that cometh in the name of our Lord.
117.26: We have blessed you of the house of our Lord:
117.27: our Lord is God, and he hath given light to us. Appoint a solemn day with thick boughs, even to the horn of the altar.
117.28: Thou art my God, and I will confess to thee: thou art my God, and I will exalt thee. I will confess to thee because thou hast heard me: and art become my salvation.
117.29: Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.

118.1: Alleluia. Aleph. Doctrine. Blessed are the immaculate in the way: which walk in the law of our Lord.
118.2: Blessed are they that search his testimonies: that seek after him with all their heart.
118.3: For they that work iniquity, have not walked in his ways.
118.4: Thou hast very much commanded thy commandments to be kept.
118.5: Would God my ways might be directed, to keep thy justifications.
118.6: Then shall I not be confounded, when I shall look throughly in all thy commandments.
118.7: I will confess to thee in direction of heart: in that I have learned the judgments of thy justice.
118.8: I will keep thy justifications: forsake me not wholly.
118.9: Beth. House. Wherein doth a young man correct his way? In keeping thy words.
118.10: With my whole heart I have sought after thee: repel me not from thy commandments.
118.11: In my heart I have hid thy words: that I may not sin to thee.
118.12: Blessed art thou, o Lord: teach me thy justifications.
118.13: In my lips I have pronounced all the judgments of thy mouth.
118.14: I am delighted in the way of thy testimonies, as in all riches.
118.15: I will be exercised in thy commandments: and I will consider thy ways.
118.16: I will meditate in thy justifications: I will not forget thy words.
118.17: Gimel. Fullness. Render to thy servant, quicken me: and I shall keep thy words.
118.18: Reveal mine eyes: and I shall consider the marvelous things of thy law.
118.19: I am a sojourner in the land, hide not thy commandments from me.
118.20: My soul hath coveted to desire thy justifications, at all time.
118.21: Thou hast rebuked the proud: cursed are they that decline from thy commandments.
118.22: Take from me reproach, and contempt: because I have sought after thy testimonies.
118.23: For Princes sat, and they spake against me: but thy servant was exercised in thy justifications.
118.24: For both thy testimonies are my meditation: and thy justifications my counsel.
118.25: Daleth. Of Tables. My soul hath cleaved to the pavement: quicken me according to thy word.
118.26: I have uttered my ways, and thou hast heard me: teach me thy justifications.
118.27: Instruct me the way of thy justifications: and I shall be exercised in thy marvelous works.
118.28: My soul hath slumbered for tediousness: confirm me in thy words.
118.29: Remove from me the way of iniquity: and according to thy law, have mercy on me.
118.30: I have chosen the way of truth: I have not forgotten thy judgments.
118.31: I have cleaved to thy testimonies, o Lord: do not confound me.
118.32: I ran the way of thy commandments: when thou didst dilate my heart.
118.33: He. This thing. Set me a law, o Lord, the way of thy justifications: and I will seek after it always.
118.34: Give me understanding, and I will search thy law: and I will keep it with my whole heart.
118.35: Conduct me into the path of thy commandments: because I would it.
118.36: Incline my heart into thy testimonies: and not into avarice.
118.37: Turn away mine eyes that they see not vanity: in thy way quicken me.
118.38: Establish thy word to thy servant, in thy fear.
118.39: Take away my reproach, which I have feared: because thy judgments are pleasant.
118.40: Behold I have coveted thy commandments: in thine equity quicken me.
118.41: Vau. And. And let thy mercy come upon me, o Lord: thy salvation according to thy word.
118.42: And I shall answer a word to them that upbraid me: because I have hoped in thy words.
118.43: And take not away out of my mouth the word of truth utterly: because I have much hoped in thy judgments.
118.44: And I will keep thy law always: for ever, and for ever and ever.
118.45: And I walked in largeness: because I have sought after thy commandments.
118.46: And I spake of thy testimonies in the sight of kings: and was not confounded.
118.47: And I meditated in thy commandments, which I loved.
118.48: And I have lifted up my hands to thy commandments, which I loved: and I was exercised in thy justifications.
118.49: Zain. This. Be mindful of thy word to thy servant, wherein thou hast given me hope.
118.50: This hath comforted me in my humiliation: because thy word hath quickened me.
118.51: The proud did unjustly exceedingly: but I declined not from thy law.
118.52: I have been mindful of thy judgments from everlasting, o Lord: and was comforted.
118.53: Fainting possessed me, because of sinners forsaking thy Law.
118.54: Thy justifications were song by me, in the place of my peregrination.
118.55: I have been mindful in the night of thy name, o Lord: and have kept thy law.
118.56: This was done to me: because I sought after thy justifications.
118.57: Heth. Life. My portion, o Lord, I said to keep thy law.
118.58: I besought thy face, with all my heart: have mercy on me according to thy word.
118.59: I thought upon my ways: and converted my feet unto thy testimonies.
118.60: I am prepared, and am not troubled: to keep thy commandments.
118.61: The cords of sinners have wrapped me round about: and I have not forgotten thy law.
118.62: At mid-night I rose to confess to thee, for the judgments of thy justification.
118.63: I am partaker of all that fear thee: and that keep thy commandments.
118.64: The earth, o Lord, is full of thy mercy: teach me thy justifications.
118.65: Teth. Good. Thou hast done bounty with thy servant, o Lord: according to thy word.
118.66: Teach me goodness, and discipline, and knowledge: because I have believed thy commandments.
118.67: Before I was humbled I offended: therefore have I kept thy word.
118.68: Thou art good: and in thy goodness teach me thy justifications.
118.69: The iniquity of the proud is multiplied upon me: but I in all my heart will search thy commandments.
118.70: Their heart is curded together as milk: but I have meditated thy law.
118.71: It is good for me that thou hast humbled me: that I may learn thy justifications.
118.72: The law of thy mouth is good unto me, above thousands of gold and silver.
118.73: Iod. Beginning. Thy hands have made me, and formed me: give me understanding, and I will learn thy commandments.
118.74: They that fear thee shall see me, & shall rejoice: because I have much hoped in thy words.
118.75: I know, o Lord, that thy judgments are equity: and in thy truth thou hast humbled me.
118.76: Let thy mercy be done to comfort me, according to thy word unto thy servant.
118.77: Let thy commiserations come to me, and I shall live: because thy law is my meditation.
118.78: Let the proud be confounded, because they have done unjustly toward me: but I will be exercised in thy commandments.
118.79: Let them be converted to me that fear thee: and that know thy testimonies.
118.80: Let my heart be made immaculate in thy justifications, that I be not confounded.
118.81: Caph. Hand, or Palm of the hand. My soul hath fainted for thy salvation: and I have much hoped in thy word.
118.82: Mine eyes have failed for thy word, saying: When wilt thou comfort me?
118.83: Because I am made as a bottle in the hoar frost: I have not forgotten thy justifications.
118.84: How many are the days of thy servant: when wilt thou do judgment on them that persecute me?
118.85: The unjust have told me fables: but not as thy law.
118.86: All thy commandments are truth: they have unjustly persecuted me, help me.
118.87: They have well near made end of me in the earth: but I have not forsaken thy commandments.
118.88: According to thy mercy quicken me: and I shall keep the testimonies of thy mouth.
118.89: Lamed. Discipline. For ever Lord thy word is permanent in heaven.
118.90: Thy truth in generation and generation: thou hast founded the earth, and it is permanent.
118.91: By thy ordinance the day continueth: because all things serve thee.
118.92: But that thy law is my meditation: I had then perhaps perished in my humiliation.
118.93: I will not forget thy justifications for ever: because in them thou hast quickened me.
118.94: I am thine, save me: because I have sought out thy justifications.
118.95: Sinners have expected me to destroy me: I understood thy testimonies.
118.96: Of all consummation I have seen the end: thy commandment is exceeding large.
118.97: Mem. Of them. How have I loved thy law, o Lord! All the day it is my meditation.
118.98: Above mine enemies thou hast made me wise by thy commandment: because it is to me for ever.
118.99: Above all that taught me have I understood: because thy testimonies are my meditation.
118.100: Above ancients have I understood: because I have sought thy commandments.
118.101: I have stayed my feet from all evil way: that I may keep thy words.
118.102: I have not declined from thy judgments: because thou hast set me a law.
118.103: How sweet are thy words to my jaws. More than honey to my mouth!
118.104: By thy commandments I have understood: therefore have I hated all the way of iniquity.
118.105: Nun. Everlasting. Thy word is a lamp to my feet, and a light to my paths.
118.106: I sware, and have determined to keep the judgments of thy justice.
118.107: I am humbled exceedingly, o Lord: quicken me according to thy word.
118.108: The voluntaries of my mouth make acceptable, o Lord: and teach me thy judgments.
118.109: My soul is in my hands always: and I have not forgotten thy law.
118.110: Sinners laid a snare for me: and I have not erred from thy commandments.
118.111: For inheritance I have purchased thy testimonies for ever: because they are the joy of my heart.
118.112: I have inclined my heart to do thy justifications for ever, for reward.
118.113: Samech. Help. I have hated the unjust: and I have loved thy law.
118.114: Thou art my helper, and protector: and upon thy word I have much hoped.
118.115: Depart from me ye malignant: and I will search the commandments of my God.
118.116: Receive me according to thy word, and I shall live: and confound me not of mine expectation.
118.117: Help me, and I shall be saved: and I will meditate in thy justifications always.
118.118: Thou hast despised all that revolt from thy judgments: because their cogitation is unjust.
118.119: All the sinners of the earth I have reputed prevaricators: therefore have I loved thy testimonies.
118.120: Pierce my flesh with thy fear: for I am afraid of thy judgments.
118.121: Ain. A fountain, or an eye. I have done judgment and justice: deliver me not to them that calumniate me.
118.122: Receive thy servant unto good: let not the proud calumniate me.
118.123: Mine eyes have failed after thy salvation: and for the word of thy justice.
118.124: Do with thy servant according to thy mercy: and teach me thy justifications.
118.125: I am thy servant: give me understanding, that I may know thy testimonies.
118.126: It is time to do, o Lord: they have dissipated thy law.
118.127: Therefore have I loved thy commandments, above gold and topazius.
118.128: Therefore was I directed to all thy commandments: all wicked way I have hated.
118.129: Phe. Mouth. Thy testimonies are marvelous: therefore hath my soul searched them.
118.130: The declaration of thy words doth illuminate: and giveth understanding to little ones.
118.131: I opened my mouth, and drew breath: because I desired thy commandments.
118.132: Look upon me, and have mercy on me, according to the judgment of them that love thy name.
118.133: Direct my steps according to thy word: and let not any injustice have domination over me.
118.134: Redeem me from the calumnies of men: that I may keep thy commandments.
118.135: Illuminate thy face upon thy servant: and teach me thy justifications.
118.136: Mine eyes have gushed forth issues of waters: because they have not kept thy law.
118.137: Sade. Justice. Thou art just, o Lord: and thy judgment is right.
118.138: Thou hast commanded justice thy testimonies: and thy verity exceedingly.
118.139: My zeal hath made me to pine away: because mine enemies have forgotten thy words.
118.140: Thy word is fired exceedingly: and thy servant hath loved it.
118.141: I am a youngman, and contemned: I have not forgotten thy justifications.
118.142: Thy justice is justice for ever: and thy law is verity.
118.143: Tribulation and distress have found me: thy commandments are my meditation.
118.144: Thy testimonies are equity for ever give me understanding, and I shall live.
118.145: Coph. Vocation. I have cried in my whole heart, hear me, o Lord: I will seek after thy justifications.
118.146: I have cried to thee, save me: that I may keep thy commandments.
118.147: I have prevented in maturity, and have cried: because I hoped much in thy words.
118.148: Mine eyes have prevented early unto thee: that I might meditate thy words.
118.149: Hear my voice according to thy mercy, o Lord: and according to thy judgment quicken me.
118.150: They that persecute me have approached to iniquity: but from thy law they are made far off.
118.151: Thou art nigh, o Lord: and all thy ways are truth.
118.152: From the beginning I knew of thy testimonies: that thou hast founded them for ever.
118.153: Res. Head. See my humiliation, and deliver me: because I have not forgotten thy law.
118.154: Judge my judgment, & redeem me: for thy word quicken thou me.
118.155: Salvation is far from sinners: because they have not sought after thy justifications.
118.156: Thy mercies are many, o Lord: according to thy judgment quicken me.
118.157: There are many that persecute me, and afflict me: I have not declined from thy testimonies.
118.158: Isaw the prevaricators, and I pined away: because they kept not thy words.
118.159: See that I have loved thy commandments, o Lord: in thy mercy quicken me.
118.160: The beginning of thy words is truth: all the judgments of thy justice are for ever.
118.161: Sin. Tooth. Princes have persecuted me without cause: and my heart hath been afraid of thy words.
118.162: I will rejoice at thy words: as he that findeth many spoils.
118.163: I have heard iniquity, and abhorred it: but thy law I have loved.
118.164: Seven times in the day I have said praise to thee, for the judgments of thy justice.
118.165: There is much peace to them that love thy law: & there is no scandal to them.
118.166: I expected thy salvation, o Lord: and have loved thy commandments.
118.167: My soul hath kept thy testimonies: & hath loved them exceedingly.
118.168: I have kept thy commandments, and thy testimonies: because all my ways are in thy sight.
118.169: Tau. Sign. Let my petition approach in thy sight, o Lord: according to thy word give me understanding.
118.170: Let my request enter in thy sight: according to thy word deliver me.
118.171: My lips shall utter an hymn, when thou shalt teach me thy justifications.
118.172: My tongue shall pronounce thy word: because all thy commandments are equity.
118.173: Let thy hand be to save me: because I have chosen thy commandments.
118.174: I have coveted thy salvation, o Lord: and thy law is my meditation.
118.175: My soul shall live, and shall praise thee: and thy judgments shall help me.
118.176: I have strayed as a sheep that is lost: seek thy servant, because I have not forgotten thy commandments.

119.1: Agradual Canticle. When I was in tribulation I cried to our Lord: and he heard me.
119.2: O Lord deliver my soul from unjust lips, and from a deceitful tongue.
119.3: What may be given thee, or what may be added unto thee to a deceitful tongue?
119.4: The sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of desolation.
119.5: Woe is to me, that my sojourning is prolonged: I have dwelt with the inhabitants of Cedar:
119.6: My soul hath been long a sojourner.
119.7: With them that hated peace I was peaceable: when I spake to them, they impugned me without cause.

120.1: Agradual Canticle. I have lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, from whence help shall come to me.
120.2: My help is from our Lord, who made heaven and earth.
120.3: Give he not thy foot to be moved: neither do he slumber that keepeth thee.
120.4: Lo he shall not slumber nor sleep, that keepeth Israel.
120.5: Our Lord keepeth thee, our Lord is thy protection, upon thy right hand.
120.6: By day the sun shall not burn thee: nor the moon by night.
120.7: Our Lord doth keep thee from all evil: our Lord keep thy soul.
120.8: Our Lord keep thy coming in, and thy going out: from hence forth now, and for ever.

121.1: Agradual Canticle. I rejoiced in these things, which were said to me: We shall go into the house of our Lord.
121.2: Our feet were standing, in thy courts, o Jerusalem.
121.3: Jerusalem, which is built as a city: whose participation is together in it-self.
121.4: For thither did the tribes ascend, the tribes of our Lord: the testimony of Israel to confess unto the name of our Lord.
121.5: Because seats sat there in judgment, seats upon the house of David.
121.6: Ask ye the things that are for the peace of Jerusalem: and aboundance to them that love thee.
121.7: Peace be made in thy strength: and aboundance in thy towers.
121.8: For my brethren, and my neighbours' sakes, I spake peace of thee:
121.9: For the house of our Lord God, I have sought good things to thee.

122.1: Agradual Canticle. To thee have I lifted up mine eyes, which dwellest in the heavens.
122.2: Behold as the eyes of servants, are on the hands of their masters, As the eyes of the handmaid on the hands of her mistress: so are our eyes to our Lord God until he have mercy on us.
122.3: Have mercy on us, o Lord, have mercy on us: because we are much replenished with contempt.
122.4: Because our soul is much replenished: reproach to them that abound, and contempt to the proud.

123.1: Agradual Canticle. But that our Lord was in us, let Israel now say,
123.2: but that our Lord was in us. When men rose up against us,
123.3: perhaps they had swallowed us alive: When their fury was angry against us,
123.5: Our soul hath passed through a torrent: perhaps our soul had passed through an intolerable water.
123.6: Blessed be our Lord which hath not given us for a prey to their teeth.
123.7: Our soul as a sparrow is delivered from the snare of the fowlers: The snare is broken, and we are delivered.
123.8: Our help is in the name of our Lord, who made heaven and earth.

124.1: Agradual Canticle. They that trust in our Lord, as mount Sion: he shall not be moved for ever that dwelleth
124.2: in Jerusalem. Mountains round about it: and our Lord round about his people, from hence forth now and for ever.
124.3: Because our Lord will not leave the rod of sinners upon the lot of the just: that the just reach not their hands to iniquity.
124.4: Do well, o Lord, to the good, and right of heart.
124.5: But those that decline into obligations, our Lord will bring with them that work iniquity: peace upon Israel.

125.1: Agradual Canticle. When our Lord turned the captivity of Sion: we were made as men comforted.
125.2: Then was our mouth replenished with joy: and our tongue with exultation. Then shall they say among the Gentiles: Our Lord hath done magnifically with them.
125.3: Our Lord hath done magnifically with us: we are made joyful.
125.4: Turn our captivity, o Lord, as a torrent in the South.
125.5: They that sow in tears, shall reap in joyfulness.
125.6: Going they went and wept, casting their seeds. But coming they shall come with exultation, carrying their sheaves.

126.1: Agradual Canticle of Salomon. Unless our Lord build the house, they have laboured in vain that build it. Unless our Lord keep the city, he watcheth in vain that keepeth it.
126.2: It is vain for you to rise before light: rise ye after ye have sitten, which eat the bread of sorrow.
126.3: When he shall give sleep to his beloved: behold the inheritance of our Lord are children: the reward, the fruit of the womb.
126.4: As arrows in the hand of the mighty: so are the children of them that are shaken.
126.5: Blessed is the man that hath filled his desire of them: he shall not be confounded when he shall speak to his enemies in the gate.

127.1: Agradual Canticle. Blessed are all that fear our Lord, that walk in his ways.
127.2: Because thou shalt eat the labours of thy hands: blessed art thou, and it shall be well with thee.
127.3: Thy wife as a fruitful vine, in the sides of thy house. Thy children as young plants of olive-trees, round about thy table.
127.4: Behold so shall the man be blessed, that feareth our Lord.
127.5: Our Lord out of Sion bless thee: and that thou mayst see the good things of Jerusalem all the days of thy life.
127.6: And that thou mayst see thy children's children, peace upon Israel.

128.1: Agradual Canticle. Often have they impugned me from my youth, let Israel now say:
128.2: Often have they impugned me from my youth: but they have not prevailed against me.
128.3: Sinners have builded upon my back: they have prolonged their iniquity.
128.4: Our just Lord will cut the necks of sinners:
128.5: let them all be confounded and turned backward, that hate Sion.
128.6: Let them be made as grass in the tops of houses: which is withered before it be plucked up.
128.7: Whereof the reaper hath not filled his hand, and he that gathereth the sheaves his bosom.
128.8: And they said not that passed by: The blessing of our Lord be upon you: we have blessed you in the name of our Lord.

129.1: Agradual Canticle. From the depths I have cried to thee, o Lord:
129.2: Lord hear my voice: Let thine ears be intent to the voice of my petition.
129.3: If thou shalt observe iniquities, o Lord: Lord who shall sustain it?
129.4: Because with thee there is propitiation: and for thy law I have expected thee, o Lord. My soul hath expected in his word:
129.5: my soul hath hoped in our Lord.
129.6: From the morning watch even until night: let Israel hope in our Lord.
129.7: Because with our Lord there is mercy: and with him plenteous redemption.
129.8: And he shall redeem Israel, from all his iniquities.

130.1: Agradual Canticle of David. Lord my heart is not exalted: neither are mine eyes lofty. Neither have I walked in great matters: nor in marvelous things above me.
130.2: If I was not humbly minded: but exalted my soul: As the weaned child is toward his mother, so retribution in my soul.
130.3: Let Israel hope in our Lord, from henceforth now and for ever.

131.1: Agradual Canticle. Remember David, o Lord, and all his meekness:
131.2: As he sware to our Lord, vowed a vow to the God of Jacob.
131.3: If I shall enter into the tabernacle of my house, if I shall ascend into the bed of my couch.
131.4: If I shall give sleep to mine eyes, and slumbering to mine eye lids:
131.5: And rest to my temples: until I find a place for our Lord, a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
131.6: Behold we have heard of it in Ephrata: we have found it in the fields of the wood.
131.7: We will enter into his tabernacle: we will adore in the place where his feet stood.
131.8: Arise Lord into thy rest, thou, and the ark of thy sanctification.
131.9: Let thy Priests be clothed with justice: & let thy saints rejoice.
131.10: For David thy servant's sake, turn not away the face of thy Christ.
131.11: Our Lord hath sworn truth to David, and he will not disappoint it: Of the fruit of thy womb I will set upon thy seat.
131.12: If thy children shall keep my testament, and these my testimonies which I will teach them: Their children also even for ever, shall sit upon thy seat.
131.13: Because our Lord hath chosen Sion: he hath chosen it for an habitation to himself.
131.14: This is my rest for ever and ever: here will I dwell because I have chosen it.
131.15: Blessing I will bless her widow: her poor I will fill with breads.
131.16: Her Priests I will clothe with salvation: and her saints shall rejoice with joyfulness.
131.17: Thither will I bring forth a horn to David, I have prepared a lamp to my Christ.
131.18: His enemies I will clothe with confusion: but upon him shall my sanctification flourish.

132.1: Agradual Canticle of David. Behold how good and how pleasant a thing it is, for brethren to dwell in one.
132.2: As ointment on the head, which ran down upon the beard, the beard of Aaron, which ran down unto the hem of his garment.
132.3: As the dew of Hermon, which runneth down upon mount Sion. Because there hath our Lord commanded blessing, and life even for ever.

133.1: Agradual Canticle. Lo now bless our Lord, all ye the servants of our Lord: Which stand in the house of our Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
133.2: In the nights lift up your hands unto the holy places, and bless ye our Lord.
133.3: Our Lord out of Sion bless thee, who made heaven and earth.

134.1: Alleluia. Praise ye the name of our Lord, ye servants praise our Lord.
134.2: Ye that stand in the house of our Lord, in the courts of the house of our God.
134.3: Praise ye our Lord, because our Lord is good: sing ye to his name, because it is sweet.
134.4: Because our Lord hath chosen Jacob to himself, Israel for his own possession.
134.5: Because I have known that our Lord is great, and our God above all gods.
134.6: All things whatsoever our Lord would he hath done, in heaven, in earth, in the sea, and in all the depths.
134.7: Bring forth clouds from the uttermost of the earth: lightnings he hath turned into rain. Who bringeth forth the winds out of his treasures:
134.8: who struck the first begotten of Ægypt from man even to beast.
134.9: He sent forth signs and wonders in the midst of thee, o Ægypt: upon Pharao, and upon all his servants.
134.10: Who struck many nations: and slew strong kings:
134.11: Sehon the king of the Amorrheites, & Og the king of Basan, and all the Kingdoms of Chanaan.
134.12: And he gave their land for inheritance, for an inheritance to Israel his people.
134.13: Lord thy name is for ever: Lord thy memorial is unto generation and generation.
134.14: Because our Lord shall judge his people: and will be entreated toward his servants.
134.15: The idols of the Gentiles are silver, and gold, the works of men's hands.
134.16: They have mouth, & shall not speak: they have eyes, and shall not see.
134.17: They have ears, and shall not hear: for neither is there breath in their mouth.
134.18: Let them that make them become like to them: and all that have confidence in them.
134.19: Ye house of Israel bless our Lord: ye house of Aaron bless our Lord.
134.20: Ye house of Levi bless our Lord: you that fear our Lord, bless ye our Lord.
134.21: Blessed be our Lord out of Sion, who dwelleth in Jerusalem.

135.1: Alleluia. Confess ye to our Lord because he is good: because his mercy is for ever.
135.2: Confess ye to the God of gods: because his mercy is for ever.
135.3: Confess ye to the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever
135.4: Who only doth great marvels: because his mercy is for ever.
135.5: Who made the heaven in understanding: because his mercy is for ever.
135.6: Who established the earth over the waters: because his mercy is for ever.
135.7: Who made the great lights: because his mercy is for ever.
135.8: The sun to rule the day: because his mercy is for ever.
135.9: The moon and stars to rule the night: because his mercy is for ever.
135.10: Who struck Ægypt with their first-begotten: because his mercy is for ever.
135.11: Who brought forth Israel out of the midst of them: because his mercy is for ever.
135.12: In a mighty hand, and lofty arm: because his mercy is for ever.
135.13: Who divided the Red sea into divisions: because his mercy is for ever.
135.14: And brought forth Israel through the midst thereof: because his mercy is for ever.
135.15: And he overthrew Pharao, and his host in the Red sea: because his mercy is for ever.
135.16: Who led his people through the desert: because his mercy is for ever.
135.17: Who struck great kings: because his mercy is for ever.
135.18: And slew strong kings: because his mercy is for ever.
135.19: Sehon the king of the Amorrheites: because his mercy is for ever.
135.20: And Og the king of Basan: because his mercy is for ever:
135.21: And he gave their land for an inheritance: because his mercy is for ever.
135.22: For an inheritance to Israel his servant: because his mercy is for ever.
135.23: For in our humiliation he was mindful of us: because his mercy is for ever.
135.24: And he redeemed us from our enemies: because his mercy is for ever.
135.25: Who giveth food to all flesh: because his mercy is for ever.
135.26: Confess ye to the God of heaven: because his mercy is for ever. Confess ye to the Lord of lords: because his mercy is for ever.

136.1: A Psalm of David for Jeremy. Upon the rivers of Babylon, there we sat and wept: whilst we remembered Sion.
136.2: On the willows in the midst thereof, we hanged up our instruments.
136.3: Because there they that led us captive, demanded of us words of songs. And they that led us away: Sing ye an hymn to us of the songs of Sion.
136.4: How shall we sing the song of our Lord in a strange land?
136.5: If I shall forget thee, o Jerusalem, let my right hand be forgotten.
136.6: Let my tongue cleave to my jaws, if I do not remember thee: If I shall not set Jerusalem in the beginning of my joy.
136.7: Be mindful, o Lord, of the children of Edom, in the day of Jerusalem: That say: Rase it, rase it, even unto the foundation thereof.
136.8: Daughter of Babylon miserable: blessed is he, that shall repay thee thy payment, which thou hast payed us.
136.9: Blessed is he that shall hold, and shall dash thy little ones against the rock.

137.1: To David himself. I will confess to thee, o Lord, in my whole heart: because thou hast heard the words of my mouth. In the sight of Angels I will sing to thee:
137.2: I will adore toward thy holy temple, and will confess to thy name: For thy mercy, and thy truth: because thou hast magnified above every thing thy holy name.
137.3: In what day soever I shall invocate thee, hear me: thou wilt multiply strength in my soul.
137.4: Let all the kings of the earth, o Lord, confess to thee: because they have heard all the words of thy mouth:
137.5: And let them sing in the ways of our Lord: because great is the glory of our Lord.
137.6: Because our Lord is high, and he beholdeth low things: and high things he knoweth far off.
137.7: If I shall walk in the midst of tribulation, thou wilt quicken me: and upon the wrath of mine enemies thou hast extended thy hand, and thy right hand hath saved me.
137.8: Our Lord will repay for me: o Lord thy mercy is for ever: despise not the works of thy hands.

138.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David. Lord thou hast proved me, and hast known me:
138.2: thou hast known my sitting down, and my rising up.
138.3: Thou hast understood my cogitations far off: my path, and my cord thou hast searched out.
138.4: And thou hast foreseen all my ways: because there is not a word in my tongue.
138.5: Behold, o Lord, thou hast known all the last things, & them of old: thou hast formed me, and hast put thy hand upon me.
138.6: Thy knowledge is become marvelous of me: it is made great, and I cannot reach to it.
138.7: Whither shall I go from thy spirit? And whither shall I fly from thy face?
138.8: If I shall ascend into heaven, thou art there: if I descend into hell, thou art present.
138.9: If I shall take my wings early, and dwell in the extreme parts of the sea.
138.10: Certes thither also shall thy hand conduct me: and thy right hand shall hold me.
138.11: And I said: Perhaps darkness shall tread over me: and the night is mine illumination in my delights.
138.12: For darkness shall not be darkened from thee, and the night shall be lightened as the day: as the darkness thereof, so also the light thereof.
138.13: Because thou hast possessed my reins: thou hast received me from my mother's womb.
138.14: I will confess to thee, because thou art terribly magnified: thy works are marvelous, & my soul knoweth exceedingly.
138.15: My bone is not hid from thee, which thou madest in secret: and my substance in the lower parts of the earth.
138.16: Mine imperfection thine eyes have seen, & in thy book all shall be written: days shall be formed, & no man in them.
138.17: But to me thy friends, o God, are become honourable exceedingly: their principality is exceedingly strengthened.
138.18: I will number them, and they shall be multiplied above the sand: I rose up and I am yet with thee.
138.19: If thou shalt kill sinners, o God: ye men of blood depart from me.
138.20: Because you say in thought: they shall receive thy cities in vain.
138.21: Did not I hate them that hate thee, o Lord: and pined away because of thine enemies?
138.22: with perfect hatred did I hate them: they are become enemies to me.
138.23: Prove me, o God, and know my heart: examine me, and know my paths.
138.24: And see, if the way of iniquity be in me: and conduct me in the everlasting way.

139.1: Unto the end, a Psalm of David.
139.2: Deliver me, o Lord, from the evil man: from the unjust man rescue me.
139.3: Which have devised iniquity in their heart: all the day they did appoint battles. They have whet their tongues as that of a serpent:
139.4: the venom of asps is under their lips.
139.5: Keep me, o Lord, from the hand of the sinner: and from unjust men deliver me.
139.6: Who have devised to supplant my steps: the proud have hid a snare for me: And they have stretched out ropes for a snare: they have laid a stumbling block for me near the way.
139.7: I said to our Lord: Thou art my God: hear, o Lord, the voice of my petition.
139.8: O Lord, Lord the strength of my salvation: thou hast overshadowed my head in the day of battle.
139.9: Yield me not, o Lord, from my desire to the sinner: they have devised against me, forsake me not, lest they perhaps be proud.
139.10: The head of their compass: the labour of their lips shall cover them.
139.11: Coals shall fall upon them, thou shalt cast them down into fire: in miseries they shall not stand up.
139.12: Aman full of tongue shall not be directed in the earth: evils shall take the unjust man into destruction.
139.13: I have known that our Lord will do the judgment of the needy: and the revenge of the poor.
139.14: But as for the just, they shall confess to thy name: and the righteous shall dwell with thy countenance.

140.1: A Psalm of David. Lord I have cried to thee, hear me: attend to my voice when I shall cry to thee.
140.2: Let my prayer be directed as incense in thy sight: the elevation of my hands as evening sacrifice.
140.3: Set o Lord a watch to my mouth: and a door round about to my lips.
140.4: Decline not my heart into words of malice, to make excuses in sins. With men that work iniquity: and I will not communicate with the chief of them.
140.5: The just shall rebuke me in mercy, and shall reprehend me: but let not the oil of a sinner fat my head. Because yet also my prayer is in their good pleasures:
140.6: their judges are swallowed up joined to the rock. They shall hear my words because they have prevailed:
140.7: as the grossness of the earth is broken out upon the earth. Our bones are dissipated near to hell:
140.8: for to thee, o Lord, Lord are mine eyes; in thee have I hoped, take not away my soul.
140.9: Keep me from the snare, which they have set for me: and from the scandals of them that work iniquity.
140.10: Sinners shall fall in his net: I am alone until I pass.

141.1: Of understanding to David, when he was in the cave, a prayer.
141.2: With my voice I have cried to our Lord: with my voice I have prayed to our Lord:
141.3: I pour out my prayer in his sight, and I pronounce my tribulation before him.
141.4: When my spirit faileth of myself, and thou hast known my paths. In this way, which I walked, they hid a snare for me.
141.5: I looked toward the right hand, and saw: and there was none that would know me. Flight hath failed me: and there is none to require my soul.
141.6: I have cried to thee, o Lord, I have said: Thou art my hope, my portion in the land of the living.
141.7: Attend to my petition: because I am humbled exceedingly. Deliver me from them that persecute me: because they are made strong over me.
141.8: Bring forth my soul out of prison, to confess unto thy name: the just expect me, till thou reward me.

142.1: A Psalm of David, when Absalom his son persecuted him. Lord hear my prayer: with thine ears receive my petition in thy truth: hear me in thy justice.
142.2: And enter not into judgment with thy servant: because no man living shall be justified in thy sight.
142.3: Because the enemy hath persecuted my soul: he hath humbled my life in the earth. He hath set me in obscure places as the dead of the world:
142.4: and my spirit is in anguish upon me, within me my heart is troubled.
142.5: I was mindful of old days, I have meditated in all thy works: in the facts of thy hands did I meditate.
142.6: I have stretched forth my hands to thee: my soul is as earth without water unto thee.
142.7: Hear me quickly, o Lord: my spirit hath fainted. Turn not away thy face from me: and I shall be like to them that descend into the lake.
142.8: Make me hear thy mercy in the morning: because I have hoped in thee. Make the way known to me, wherein I may walk: because I have lifted up my soul to thee.
142.9: Deliver me from mine enemies, o Lord, to thee I have fled:
142.10: teach me to do thy will, because thou art my God. Thy good spirit will conduct me into the right way:
142.11: for thy name sake, o Lord, thou wilt quicken me in thine equity. Thou wilt bring forth my soul out of tribulation:
142.12: and in thy mercy thou wilt destroy mine enemies. And thou wilt destroy all that afflict my soul: because I am thy servant.

143.1: A Psalm of David, against Goliath. Blessed be our Lord my God, who teacheth my hands to battle, and my fingers to war.
143.2: My mercy, and my refuge: my defender, and my deliverer. My protector; and I have hoped in him, who subdueth my people under me.
143.3: Lord what is man, that thou art made known to him? Or the son of man, that thou esteemest him?
143.4: Man is made like to vanity: his days pass as a shadow.
143.5: Lord incline thy heavens, and descend: touch the mountains, and they will smoke.
143.6: Lighten lightning, and thou shalt disperse them: shoot out thine arrows, and thou shalt destroy them.
143.7: Send forth thy hand from on high, take me out, and deliver me from many waters: from the hand of children strangers.
143.8: Whose mouth hath spoken vanity: and their right hand is the right hand of iniquity.
143.9: O God I will sing to thee a new song: in the psalter of ten strings, I will sing to thee.
143.10: Who givest salvation to kings: who hast redeemed David thy servant from the malignant sword:
143.11: deliver me. And rescue me out of the hand of children strangers, whose mouth hath spoken vanity: and their right hand, is the right hand of iniquity.
143.12: Whose sons, are as new plants in their youth. Their daughters comely trimmed: decked about after the similitude of a temple.
143.13: Their store-houses full, flowing out of this into that. Their ewes full of young, abounding in their going forth:
143.14: their oxen are fat. There is no ruin of wall, nor passage, nor cry in their streets.
143.15: They have said, that it is a happy people, which hath these things: blessed is the people, whose God is our Lord.

144.1: Praising, to David himself. I will exalt thee my God the King: and I will bless thy name for ever and for ever.
144.2: Every day will I bless thee: and will praise thy name for ever, and for ever and ever.
